attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.safety_stock. Business justification: In consumer goods MRP, planned orders are triggered by safety stock replenishment signals. This FK links the planned order to the safety stock record that triggered it, enabling safety stock coverage

attributes.name: sales_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: MRP demand pegging in make-to-order consumer goods requires planned orders to reference the triggering sales order. IBP/MRP planners use this link for order-to-production traceability and ATP confirma

attributes.name: sku_id
attributes.description: Reference to the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) that this planned order is for. Links to the product master data.

attributes.name: trade_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.trade_account. Business justification: Supports demand‑planning reports linking planned production to specific customer accounts and forecast agreements.

attributes.name: trade_promotion_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to promotion.trade_promotion. Business justification: Planned Promotional Production aligns MRP planned orders with upcoming trade promotions to ensure supply availability.

attributes.name: availability_date
attributes.description: The date when the produced or procured material from this planned order is expected to be available for consumption or shipment.

attributes.name: conversion_date
attributes.description: The date when this planned order was converted to a production order. Null if not yet converted.

attributes.name: conversion_status
attributes.description: Indicates whether this planned order has been converted to a production order: NOT_CONVERTED (still in planning), PARTIALLY_CONVERTED (split conversion), FULLY_CONVERTED (completely converted).

attributes.name: created_by_user
attributes.description: User ID or system identifier of the person or automated process that created this planned order.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: System timestamp when this planned order record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: demand_source_type
attributes.description: Classification of the demand signal that triggered this planned order: FORECAST (demand planning), CUSTOMER_ORDER (firm sales order), STOCK_TRANSFER (inter-plant), SAFETY_STOCK (replenishment), PROMOTION (trade promotion demand).

attributes.name: exception_message_code
attributes.business_glossary_term: Material Requirements Planning (MRP) Exception Message Code
attributes.description: Code representing MRP exception conditions (e.g., EX01 = reschedule in, EX02 = reschedule out, EX03 = cancel, EX10 = expedite). Empty if no exception.

tag_set.value: Material Requirements Planning (MRP) Exception Message Code

attributes.name: exception_message_text
attributes.description: Human-readable description of the MRP exception condition requiring planner attention.

attributes.name: firming_date
attributes.description: The date when this planned order was firmed by a planner. Null if not yet firmed.

attributes.name: firming_indicator
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this planned order is firmed (locked from automatic MRP changes). True = firmed, False = subject to MRP rescheduling.

attributes.name: gmp_compliance_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this planned order must comply with GMP regulations. True for pharmaceutical and cosmetic products requiring ISO 22716 compliance.

attributes.name: ibp_plan_version
attributes.description: Version identifier from SAP IBP system that generated the demand signal driving this planned order. Format: IBP-YYYYMM.

attributes.name: last_modified_by_user
attributes.description: User ID or system identifier of the person or automated process that last modified this planned order.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: System timestamp when this planned order record was last updated or modified.

attributes.name: lot_size_key
attributes.description: Code indicating the lot-sizing procedure used: EX (exact lot size), HB (lot-for-lot), FX (fixed lot size), PD (periodic lot size), WM (weekly lot size).

attributes.name: mrp_controller
attributes.description: Code identifying the MRP controller or planning group responsible for this material and plant combination.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for planners to add comments, special instructions, or contextual information about this planned order.

attributes.name: order_number
attributes.description: Business-readable unique identifier for the planned order, typically system-generated following organizational numbering conventions.

attributes.name: order_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the planned order: DRAFT (initial creation), FIRMED (locked from MRP changes), RELEASED (approved for execution), CONVERTED (converted to production order), DELETED (marked for deletion), CANCELLED (cancelled before conversion).

attributes.name: order_type
attributes.description: Classification of the planned order origin: MPS (Master Production Schedule), MRP (Material Requirements Planning generated), CBP (Consumption-Based Planning), MANUAL (manually created), FORECAST (forecast-driven), SAFETY (safety stock replenishment).

attributes.name: planned_quantity
attributes.description: The quantity of the SKU planned to be produced or procured in this order, expressed in the base unit of measure.

attributes.name: planning_period
attributes.description: The planning time bucket this order belongs to, expressed as YYYY-Wnn (week), YYYY-Mnn (month), or YYYY-Qn (quarter). Used for MPS and S&OP alignment.

attributes.name: planning_strategy_group
attributes.description: Two-digit code defining the planning strategy (e.g., 10 = make-to-stock, 20 = make-to-order, 40 = planning with final assembly, 50 = planning without final assembly).

attributes.name: priority_code
attributes.description: Priority level assigned to this planned order for scheduling and resource allocation: LOW, NORMAL, HIGH, URGENT.

attributes.name: procurement_type
attributes.description: Indicates whether this planned order is for in-house production (IN_HOUSE), external procurement (EXTERNAL), or can be either (BOTH).

attributes.name: production_version
attributes.description: Identifies the specific combination of BOM (Bill of Materials) and routing to be used for production. Links to production master data.

attributes.name: reorder_point_quantity
attributes.description: The inventory level that triggers generation of a planned order for replenishment.

attributes.name: safety_stock_quantity
attributes.description: The safety stock level maintained for this SKU at this plant, influencing planned order generation.

attributes.name: schedule_version
attributes.description: Version identifier for the MPS schedule that generated or governs this planned order. Enables tracking of schedule changes over time.

attributes.name: scheduled_finish_date
attributes.description: The planned date when production or procurement activities for this order should be completed and goods available.

attributes.name: scheduled_start_date
attributes.description: The planned date when production or procurement activities for this order should begin.

attributes.name: sop_cycle_reference
attributes.business_glossary_term: Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) Cycle Reference
attributes.description: Reference to the S&OP planning cycle that this planned order aligns with. Format: SOP-YYYY-Mnn or SOP-YYYY-Qn.

tag_set.value: Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) Cycle Reference

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The unit of measure for the planned quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, L for liter). Follows ISO standard unit codes.

products.name: equipment

attributes.description: Primary key for equipment

attributes.name: company_code_id

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Equipment assets are assigned to cost centers for depreciation posting, maintenance cost absorption, and fixed asset reporting. Standard asset accounting in consumer goods manufacturing requires this

attributes.name: manufacturing_facility_id
attributes.description: Reference to the plant entity that houses the equipment.

attributes.name: parent_equipment_id
attributes.description: Self-referencing FK on equipment (parent_equipment_id)

attributes.name: production_line_id
attributes.description: Reference to the production line on which the equipment operates.

attributes.name: purchase_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Capital equipment procurement tracking: links equipment assets to their originating purchase order for capex reporting, warranty validation, and asset lifecycle management. Consumer Goods finance and

attributes.name: supplier_contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_contract. Business justification: Equipment service/maintenance contract management: links manufacturing equipment to its active supplier maintenance contract. Consumer Goods facilities track OEM service agreements per equipment asset

attributes.name: acquisition_cost
attributes.description: Purchase price paid for the equipment.

attributes.name: asset_tag
attributes.description: Internal tag or barcode used to physically label the equipment.

attributes.name: calibration_due_date
attributes.description: Scheduled date for the next calibration.

attributes.name: capacity_units
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the equipment's capacity.

attributes.name: capacity_value
attributes.description: Numeric capacity of the equipment expressed in the specified units.

attributes.name: compliance_gmp

attributes.name: currency

attributes.name: department
attributes.description: Organizational department responsible for the equipment.

attributes.name: depreciation_end_date

attributes.name: depreciation_method
attributes.description: Accounting method used to depreciate the equipment.

attributes.name: depreciation_start_date

attributes.name: description
attributes.description: Free‑form text describing the equipment's purpose and characteristics.

attributes.name: energy_consumption_kwh
attributes.description: Total energy consumed by the equipment in kilowatt‑hours.

attributes.name: equipment_status

attributes.name: equipment_type
attributes.description: Category of equipment based on function or form factor.

attributes.name: hazard_classification
attributes.description: Level of hazard associated with equipment operation.

attributes.name: installation_date
attributes.description: Date the equipment was installed and became operational.

attributes.name: last_calibration_date

attributes.name: last_maintenance_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent scheduled maintenance.

attributes.name: location_code
attributes.description: Code identifying the plant or facility where the equipment resides.

attributes.name: maintenance_cost

attributes.name: maintenance_frequency_days

attributes.name: model_number

attributes.name: mttr_hours

attributes.name: next_maintenance_due

attributes.name: oee_actual

attributes.name: oee_target
attributes.description: Target Overall Equipment Effectiveness percentage.

attributes.name: power_rating_kw
attributes.description: Maximum power consumption of the equipment in kilowatts.

attributes.name: purchase_date

attributes.name: safety_rating
attributes.description: Safety classification of the equipment.

attributes.name: serial_number
attributes.description: Unique serial number stamped on the equipment.

attributes.name: total_usage_hours
attributes.description: Cumulative operating hours since installation.

attributes.name: voltage
attributes.description: Nominal operating voltage of the equipment.

attributes.name: warranty_end_date
attributes.description: Date when the equipment warranty expires.

attributes.name: warranty_start_date
attributes.description: Date when the equipment warranty period begins.

attributes.name: weight_kg
attributes.description: Weight of the equipment in kilograms.

domains.name: supply
domains.description: Owns end-to-end supply chain planning and orchestration including demand planning, supply planning, inventory optimization, S&OP/IBP processes, DRP execution, safety stock targets, ATP/CTP calculations, supply network design, demand sensing, forecast accuracy tracking, and supply risk management. Integrates with SAP IBP.

products.name: demand_plan
products.description: Core master entity representing the demand plan across all versions, stages, and consensus outcomes within the S&OP/IBP cycle for each SKU/location/channel/planning period combination. Each record represents a specific version-point: captures version name, version type (statistical baseline/field sales overlay/marketing-adjusted/consensus/final approved), planning cycle reference, created-by persona, approval status, effective date range, planning bucket (weekly/monthly), statistical baseline quantity, commercial overlay, marketing event uplift, new product launch volume, consensus quantity, variance to statistical baseline, channel disaggregation, promotional overlays, and lifecycle stage. The consensus version represents the single agreed demand signal passed to supply planning. Enables forecast accuracy benchmarking across versions. Sourced from SAP IBP Demand Planning module.

attributes.name: demand_plan_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the demand plan record. Primary key representing a specific version-point in the S&OP/IBP cycle for a SKU/location/channel/planning period combination.

attributes.name: event_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to promotion.promotion_event. Business justification: CPG demand planners apply event-level promotional uplifts (promotional_overlay_quantity, marketing_event_uplift_quantity) to demand plans. This FK enables direct traceability from a demand plan versio

attributes.name: planning_run_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.planning_run. Business justification: Demand plans are produced during S&OP/IBP planning runs. Linking demand_plan to the planning_run that generated it enables run-level demand plan analysis, scenario comparison, and audit traceability.

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Demand plans feed S&OP financial reconciliation where volume forecasts are converted to revenue by profit center. Consumer goods finance teams require demand plan-to-profit center linkage for integrat

attributes.name: retail_store_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.retail_store. Business justification: Store-level demand planning is standard in consumer goods for DSD and VMI accounts. Demand plans are built at individual store granularity for promotional events and new product launches. Direct FK to

attributes.name: sku_id
attributes.description: Reference to the product SKU for which demand is being planned.

attributes.name: sku_planning_param_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.sku_planning_param. Business justification: Demand plans are generated using SKU-level planning parameters (forecast_model_type, planning_horizon_days, demand_pattern_type). Linking demand_plan to sku_planning_param formalizes this dependency.

attributes.name: standard_cost_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.standard_cost. Business justification: Demand plans are converted to gross margin forecasts using standard cost in consumer goods S&OP financial reviews. Linking demand plan volumes to standard cost enables automated gross profit planning

attributes.name: supply_network_node_id
attributes.description: Reference to the distribution location, warehouse, or market geography for which demand is planned.

attributes.name: trade_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.trade_account. Business justification: REQUIRED: Account‑level demand forecast used in S&OP planning; planners need trade_account context for each demand_plan.

attributes.name: trade_calendar_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to promotion.trade_calendar. Business justification: CPG demand plans are structured around trade calendar periods (planning_period_start_date, planning_period_end_date). Linking demand_plan to trade_calendar aligns demand planning cycles with promotion

attributes.name: trade_promotion_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to promotion.trade_promotion. Business justification: S&OP consensus demand planning in CPG requires tracing promotional_overlay_quantity back to the authorizing trade promotion. This link enables promotion-to-plan reconciliation reports and ensures prom

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Current approval state of the demand plan version within the S&OP governance workflow.

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this demand plan version was formally approved and locked for supply planning execution.

attributes.name: commercial_overlay_quantity
attributes.description: Demand adjustment quantity applied by field sales teams based on customer intelligence, market conditions, and sales pipeline visibility.

attributes.name: confidence_level
attributes.description: Planner-assigned confidence rating in the demand forecast based on data quality, market visibility, and forecast stability.

attributes.name: consensus_quantity
attributes.description: Agreed demand forecast quantity representing the single demand signal passed to supply planning after S&OP consensus process. This is the authoritative demand plan.

attributes.name: created_by_persona
attributes.description: Role or persona of the user who created this demand plan version, used for accountability and collaboration tracking.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this demand plan version record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for any monetary values associated with this demand plan (e.g., revenue forecasts).

attributes.name: demand_pattern_type
attributes.description: Classification of historical demand behavior pattern used to select appropriate statistical forecasting algorithm.

attributes.name: demand_sensing_signal
attributes.description: Short-term demand trend indicator derived from real-time POS data, shipment data, and market signals to enable rapid forecast adjustments.

attributes.name: effective_from_date
attributes.description: Date from which this demand plan version becomes active and is used for supply planning and ATP calculations.

attributes.name: effective_to_date
attributes.description: Date until which this demand plan version remains active. Null indicates the version is currently active with no planned expiration.

attributes.name: forecast_accuracy_percentage
attributes.description: Historical forecast accuracy metric for this SKU/location/channel combination, used for forecast quality benchmarking and continuous improvement.

attributes.name: forecast_bias_percentage
attributes.description: Systematic tendency to over-forecast or under-forecast, calculated as average percentage deviation from actual demand.

attributes.name: is_consensus_version
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this version represents the official consensus demand plan approved through the S&OP process and used for supply planning.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this demand plan version was last updated or adjusted.

attributes.name: lifecycle_stage
attributes.description: Product lifecycle stage classification used to apply appropriate forecasting models and demand shaping strategies.

attributes.name: marketing_event_uplift_quantity
attributes.description: Incremental demand quantity expected from planned marketing campaigns, promotions, and brand activation events.

attributes.name: npd_launch_volume_quantity
attributes.business_glossary_term: New Product Development (NPD) Launch Volume Quantity
attributes.description: Forecasted demand quantity for new product launches or product line extensions during the planning period.

tag_set.value: New Product Development (NPD) Launch Volume Quantity

attributes.name: planner_notes
attributes.description: Free-text comments and annotations from demand planners explaining assumptions, adjustments, risks, or special considerations for this forecast.

attributes.name: planning_bucket
attributes.description: Time granularity of the demand plan (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly) used for aggregation and reporting.

attributes.name: planning_period_end_date
attributes.description: End date of the planning period (week or month) for which demand is forecasted.

attributes.name: planning_period_start_date
attributes.description: Start date of the planning period (week or month) for which demand is forecasted.

attributes.name: promotional_overlay_quantity
attributes.description: Demand uplift quantity attributed to trade promotions, price discounts, and retail execution activities.

attributes.name: risk_category
attributes.description: Classification of the primary risk factor affecting demand plan reliability and supply fulfillment capability.

attributes.name: risk_flag
attributes.description: Indicator that this demand plan carries elevated supply risk due to capacity constraints, material shortages, or demand volatility.

attributes.name: source_system_record_code
attributes.description: Original unique identifier of this demand plan record in the source system, used for data lineage and reconciliation.

attributes.name: statistical_baseline_quantity
attributes.description: Unconstrained demand forecast quantity generated by statistical forecasting algorithms before any commercial overlays or adjustments.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Unit in which demand quantities are expressed (each, case, pallet, kilogram, liter, etc.). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PL|KG|LB|L|GAL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: variance_to_baseline_quantity
attributes.description: Difference between consensus quantity and statistical baseline quantity, indicating the net impact of all commercial adjustments and overlays.

attributes.name: version_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name of the demand plan version (e.g., 'Statistical Baseline Q1 2024', 'Consensus Final March 2024').

attributes.name: version_sequence_number
attributes.description: Sequential version number within the planning cycle, used to track iteration history and version progression.

attributes.name: version_type
attributes.description: Classification of the demand plan version indicating its stage in the S&OP process. Statistical baseline represents unconstrained forecast; consensus represents agreed demand signal passed to supply planning.

products.name: forecast_version
products.description: Tracks each named version or snapshot of the demand forecast within the IBP/S&OP cycle (e.g., statistical baseline, field sales overlay, marketing-adjusted, consensus, final approved). Captures version name, version type, planning cycle reference, created-by persona, approval status, and effective date range. Enables forecast accuracy benchmarking across versions.

attributes.name: forecast_version_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the forecast version record. Primary key.

attributes.name: baseline_forecast_version_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent or baseline forecast version from which this version was derived (e.g., statistical baseline that was then adjusted). Nullable for original baseline versions.

attributes.name: event_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to promotion.promotion_event. Business justification: CPG forecast versions incorporating promotional lifts must be traceable to the specific promotion event. This FK supports post-event forecast accuracy reporting (MAPE by promotion event) and enables d

attributes.name: planning_run_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.planning_run. Business justification: Forecast versions are snapshots created during planning runs (IBP cycles). Linking forecast_version to the planning_run that produced it enables run-level forecast version tracking and scenario analys

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Forecast versions are consumed by finance for revenue planning by profit center during budget and rolling forecast cycles. Consumer goods IBP processes require forecast version traceability to profit

attributes.name: trade_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.trade_account. Business justification: Key account forecasting is a standard consumer goods process where forecast versions are built per trade account (e.g., Walmart-specific forecast). Supply planners and key account managers jointly rev

attributes.name: trade_calendar_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to promotion.trade_calendar. Business justification: CPG forecast versions are aligned to trade calendar planning cycles (planning_horizon_start_date, planning_horizon_end_date). This FK enables forecast version management by trade calendar period, supp

attributes.name: trade_promotion_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to promotion.trade_promotion. Business justification: forecast_version has promotional_events_included flag indicating trade promotions influenced the forecast. Linking to trade_promotion enables forecast accuracy attribution by trade promotion — a stand

attributes.name: aggregation_level
attributes.description: The level of aggregation at which this forecast version is maintained: Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) level, product family, brand, category, sales channel, customer account, geographic region, or global total. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sku|product_family|brand|category|channel|customer|region|global — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: approval_notes
attributes.description: Comments or notes provided by the approver during the approval process, documenting any conditions, concerns, or guidance related to this forecast version. Nullable if not yet approved or no notes provided.

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this forecast version was approved. Nullable if not yet approved.

attributes.name: archived_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this forecast version was archived and moved out of active planning use. Nullable if not yet archived.

attributes.name: bias_percentage
attributes.description: Forecast bias percentage indicating systematic over-forecasting (positive bias) or under-forecasting (negative bias). A value near zero indicates an unbiased forecast. Nullable if not yet calculated.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this forecast version record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for any monetary values associated with this forecast version (e.g., revenue forecasts). Nullable if forecast is purely volume-based.

attributes.name: demand_sensing_applied
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether demand sensing algorithms (short-term forecast adjustments based on real-time Point of Sale (POS) data, shipment data, or other leading indicators) were applied to this forecast version.

attributes.name: effective_from_date
attributes.description: The date from which this forecast version becomes effective and is used for operational planning, Distribution Requirements Planning (DRP), and Available to Promise (ATP) calculations.

attributes.name: effective_to_date
attributes.description: The date until which this forecast version remains effective. Nullable for open-ended versions that remain active until superseded.

attributes.name: forecast_accuracy_percentage
attributes.description: Calculated forecast accuracy percentage for this version, measured as the percentage of forecasted demand that matched actual demand. Used for benchmarking version performance and continuous improvement. Nullable if accuracy has not yet been calculated.

attributes.name: forecast_source_system
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the source system or application that generated or contributed to this forecast version (e.g., 'SAP IBP', 'TradeEdge', 'Excel Upload', 'Salesforce CG Cloud').

attributes.name: is_active_version
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this forecast version is currently active and being used for planning and execution. Only one version per planning cycle should typically be active at a time.

attributes.name: is_consensus_version
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this version represents the consensus forecast agreed upon by cross-functional stakeholders (sales, marketing, supply chain, finance) during the S&OP process.

attributes.name: is_final_approved_version
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this version is the final approved forecast that will be used for operational execution, Material Requirements Planning (MRP), and Distribution Requirements Planning (DRP).

attributes.name: mean_absolute_percentage_error
attributes.description: Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) metric for this forecast version, measuring the average magnitude of forecast errors as a percentage. Lower values indicate better forecast accuracy. Nullable if not yet calculated.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this forecast version record was last modified.

attributes.name: new_product_introduction_included
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether New Product Development (NPD) or New Product Introduction (NPI) launches were incorporated into this forecast version.

attributes.name: planning_horizon_end_date
attributes.description: The last date of the planning period covered by this forecast version (e.g., end of the fiscal quarter or rolling 18-month horizon).

attributes.name: planning_horizon_start_date
attributes.description: The first date of the planning period covered by this forecast version (e.g., start of the fiscal quarter or month).

attributes.name: promotional_events_included
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether trade promotions, marketing campaigns, and promotional events were factored into this forecast version.

attributes.name: rejection_reason
attributes.description: Explanation or reason provided when this forecast version was rejected during the approval process. Nullable if version was not rejected.

attributes.name: statistical_model_applied
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the statistical forecasting model or algorithm applied to generate or adjust this forecast version (e.g., 'Exponential Smoothing', 'ARIMA', 'Machine Learning Ensemble', 'Holt-Winters'). Nullable if no statistical model was used.

attributes.name: time_bucket
attributes.description: The time granularity or bucket size used for this forecast version: daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, or annual periods.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The unit of measure in which forecast quantities are expressed (e.g., 'EA' for each, 'CS' for case, 'KG' for kilogram, 'L' for liter). Aligns with GS1 standards.

attributes.name: version_code
attributes.description: Short alphanumeric code or identifier for the forecast version used in system integrations and reporting (e.g., 'STAT_BASE', 'CONS_V1', 'FINAL_APR').

attributes.name: version_description
attributes.description: Detailed textual description or notes about this forecast version, including assumptions, adjustments made, special considerations, or rationale for key changes.

attributes.name: version_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name assigned to this forecast version (e.g., 'Statistical Baseline Q1 2024', 'Consensus Forecast March', 'Final Approved Version').

attributes.name: version_sequence_number
attributes.description: Sequential number indicating the iteration or revision order of this forecast version within the planning cycle (e.g., 1 for first draft, 2 for first revision, 3 for consensus).

attributes.name: version_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the forecast version: draft (under construction), in review (submitted for approval), approved (validated and accepted), rejected (not accepted), archived (historical record), or active (currently in use for planning).

attributes.name: version_type
attributes.description: Classification of the forecast version indicating its role in the planning process: statistical baseline (system-generated), field sales overlay (sales team input), marketing adjusted (marketing promotions incorporated), consensus (collaborative agreement), final approved (executive sign-off), or simulation (what-if scenario).

products.name: inventory_policy
products.description: Master entity defining inventory policy parameters, safety stock calculations, service level commitments, and OTIF targets for each SKU/location node in the supply network. Captures safety stock target and calculated quantities (days of supply, units, calculation method, demand variability, lead time variability, service level input, calculated vs approved SS units, effective date, review status), reorder point, min/max levels, cycle stock target, replenishment method (MRP/DRP/VMI), review cycle, service level targets (fill rate %, on-time delivery %, OTIF composite %), customer/channel-level OTIF commitments, retailer-mandated target flags, penalty clause indicators, and measurement windows. Owned by supply planning; consumed by distribution and manufacturing execution.

attributes.name: inventory_policy_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the inventory policy record. Primary key for this entity.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Inventory policies are owned by specific cost centers; linking enables policy‑related budgeting and compliance audits.

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Inventory policies define service level and stock targets that represent working capital commitments by profit center. Consumer goods finance requires inventory policy linkage to profit center for bra

attributes.name: sku_id
attributes.description: Reference to the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for which this inventory policy is defined.

attributes.name: standard_cost_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.standard_cost. Business justification: Inventory policy targets (safety_stock_target_units, maximum_stock_level_units) are financially justified using standard cost for carrying cost calculations. Consumer goods supply finance uses this li

attributes.name: supply_network_node_id
attributes.description: Reference to the distribution facility, warehouse, or storage location node in the supply network where this policy applies.

attributes.name: trade_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.trade_account. Business justification: Inventory policies in consumer goods are frequently retailer-mandated (retailer_mandated_target_flag, penalty_clause_indicator, customer_otif_commitment_percent). Supply planners must know which trade

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date when this inventory policy was formally approved and authorized for use in supply planning and execution.

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Current approval workflow status indicating whether the policy has been reviewed and approved by authorized supply planning personnel.

attributes.name: approved_by_name
attributes.description: Name of the supply planning manager or director who approved this inventory policy for operational use.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this inventory policy record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: customer_otif_commitment_percent
attributes.description: Specific OTIF performance level committed to a customer or channel, which may differ from internal targets and may be contractually binding.

attributes.name: cycle_stock_target_units
attributes.description: Target cycle stock quantity representing the average inventory held to meet expected demand between replenishments, excluding safety stock.

attributes.name: demand_variability_coefficient
attributes.description: Statistical measure of demand variability (coefficient of variation or standard deviation) used in safety stock calculations.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: Date on which this inventory policy expires or is superseded. Null indicates an open-ended policy.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: Date from which this inventory policy becomes active and applicable for planning and execution.

attributes.name: fill_rate_target_percent
attributes.description: Target percentage of customer demand that should be fulfilled from available inventory without backorders or stockouts.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this inventory policy record was last updated or modified.

attributes.name: last_review_date
attributes.description: Date when this inventory policy was last reviewed and validated by the supply planning team.

attributes.name: lead_time_variability_days
attributes.description: Standard deviation or variance of replenishment lead time in days, used to account for supply uncertainty in safety stock calculations.

attributes.name: maximum_stock_level_units
attributes.description: Maximum inventory level allowed at this location for this SKU, used to prevent overstocking and manage warehouse capacity constraints.

attributes.name: measurement_window_days
attributes.description: Time period in days over which service level performance is measured and evaluated against targets (e.g., rolling 30 days, 90 days, quarterly).

attributes.name: minimum_stock_level_units
attributes.description: Minimum inventory level that should be maintained at this location for this SKU, typically aligned with safety stock or regulatory requirements.

attributes.name: next_review_date
attributes.description: Scheduled date for the next review and update of this inventory policy, ensuring policies remain aligned with business conditions.

attributes.name: on_time_delivery_target_percent
attributes.description: Target percentage of orders that should be delivered by the customer-requested or committed delivery date.

attributes.name: otif_composite_target_percent
attributes.business_glossary_term: On Time In Full (OTIF) Composite Target Percent
attributes.description: Composite OTIF (On Time In Full) target percentage representing orders delivered both on time and in full quantity, a critical customer service metric.

tag_set.value: On Time In Full (OTIF) Composite Target Percent

attributes.name: penalty_clause_indicator
attributes.description: Indicates whether failure to meet the defined service level targets will result in financial penalties, chargebacks, or other contractual consequences.

attributes.name: policy_code
attributes.description: Business identifier code for the inventory policy, used for external reference and reporting.

attributes.name: policy_notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes capturing special considerations, exceptions, seasonal adjustments, or business context relevant to this inventory policy.

attributes.name: policy_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the inventory policy indicating its approval and operational state. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|active|suspended|expired|archived — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: reorder_point_units
attributes.description: Inventory level at which a replenishment order should be triggered, calculated as lead time demand plus safety stock.

attributes.name: replenishment_method
attributes.description: The inventory replenishment strategy applied: MRP (Material Requirements Planning), DRP (Distribution Requirements Planning), VMI (Vendor Managed Inventory), JIT (Just In Time), Kanban, or Fixed Order Quantity.

attributes.name: retailer_mandated_target_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the service level targets are mandated by a retail customer as part of their supplier requirements or vendor compliance program.

attributes.name: review_cycle_days
attributes.description: Frequency in days at which inventory levels are reviewed and replenishment decisions are made for this SKU-location combination.

attributes.name: safety_stock_calculated_units
attributes.description: System-calculated safety stock quantity based on demand variability, lead time variability, and service level inputs before manual approval or adjustment.

attributes.name: safety_stock_calculation_method
attributes.description: The methodology used to calculate safety stock: fixed value, forecast error-based, demand variability, lead time variability, combined variability, or service level-driven calculation.

attributes.name: safety_stock_days_of_supply
attributes.description: Safety stock expressed as the number of days of average demand coverage, providing a time-based view of inventory buffer.

attributes.name: safety_stock_target_units
attributes.description: Target safety stock quantity in base units of measure, representing the buffer inventory to protect against demand and supply variability.

attributes.name: service_level_target_percent
attributes.description: Target service level percentage (e.g., 95%, 98%, 99.5%) representing the desired probability of not stocking out during the replenishment lead time.

products.name: safety_stock
products.description: Transactional record capturing the calculated and approved safety stock quantity for each SKU/location/time-period combination. Stores calculation method (statistical, days-of-supply, manual override), demand variability, lead time variability, service level input, calculated SS units, approved SS units, effective date, and review status. Distinct from inventory_policy (policy target) as this captures the actual calculated output.

attributes.name: safety_stock_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the safety stock calculation record. Primary key for the safety stock entity.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Safety stock holding costs (holding_cost_per_unit, stockout_cost_per_unit) are charged to cost centers for inventory carrying cost allocation and working capital reporting. Consumer goods finance team

attributes.name: demand_plan_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.demand_plan. Business justification: Safety stock calculations are driven by demand plan inputs (average daily demand, forecast accuracy). Linking safety_stock to the governing demand_plan provides full traceability from demand signal to

attributes.name: event_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to promotion.promotion_event. Business justification: In CPG, safety stock levels are recalculated and elevated ahead of promotional events to prevent stockouts. This FK allows supply planners to trace safety stock overrides (override_reason_code, overri

attributes.name: forecast_version_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.forecast_version. Business justification: Safety stock calculations reference a specific forecast version for demand variability inputs. Linking to forecast_version enables audit traceability — which forecast snapshot drove the safety stock c

attributes.name: planning_run_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.planning_run. Business justification: Safety stock records are computed outputs of planning runs (IBP/S&OP cycles). Linking safety_stock to the planning_run that produced it enables run-level analysis and reprocessing. calculation_timesta

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Safety stock investment is tracked by profit center for brand/channel working capital management. Consumer goods CFOs report safety stock value by profit center in monthly financial reviews; this link

attributes.name: sku_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific product SKU for which safety stock is calculated. Links to the product master data.

attributes.name: sku_planning_param_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.sku_planning_param. Business justification: Safety stock calculations use SKU-level planning parameters (safety_stock_method, lead_time_days, lot_size_rule). Linking safety_stock to sku_planning_param formalizes this dependency and avoids dupli

attributes.name: standard_cost_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.standard_cost. Business justification: Safety stock financial valuation uses standard cost per unit to compute total inventory investment and holding cost. Consumer goods S&OP and working capital reports require linking safety stock quanti

attributes.name: supply_network_node_id
attributes.description: Reference to the storage location, distribution center, or facility where the safety stock applies. Links to distribution facility master data.

attributes.name: abc_classification
attributes.description: The ABC classification of the SKU based on revenue contribution or volume. A items are high-value requiring tight control; B items are moderate; C items are low-value. Influences service level targets and safety stock investment decisions.

attributes.name: approval_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when the safety stock calculation was approved by the planner. Marks the transition from calculated to approved state in the workflow.

attributes.name: approved_safety_stock_units
attributes.description: The final approved safety stock quantity in base units of measure after planner review and adjustment. This is the target value used for inventory policy execution and Available to Promise (ATP) calculations.

attributes.name: average_daily_demand_units
attributes.description: The mean daily demand quantity in base units of measure. Used as input to safety stock calculations and represents expected consumption rate.

attributes.name: average_lead_time_days
attributes.description: The mean replenishment lead time in days from order placement to receipt. Used in safety stock calculations to determine the exposure period for demand and supply variability.

attributes.name: calculated_safety_stock_units
attributes.description: The system-calculated safety stock quantity in base units of measure. This is the raw output from the calculation engine before any manual adjustments or approvals.

attributes.name: calculation_method
attributes.description: The methodology used to calculate the safety stock quantity. Statistical methods use demand and lead time variability; days-of-supply uses fixed coverage targets; manual override represents planner judgment; service level and lead time methods focus on specific risk factors; hybrid combines multiple approaches.

attributes.name: calculation_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when the safety stock calculation was executed by the planning system. Provides audit trail for calculation runs and version control.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this safety stock record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for record lifecycle tracking.

attributes.name: data_quality_score
attributes.description: Score indicating the quality of the data record

attributes.name: days_of_supply_target
attributes.description: The target safety stock coverage expressed in days of average demand. Used when calculation method is days-of-supply based. Represents the buffer duration to protect against stockouts.

attributes.name: demand_classification
attributes.description: The demand pattern category for this SKU-location. Steady indicates consistent demand; seasonal shows periodic peaks; erratic has high variability; lumpy has infrequent large orders; intermittent has sporadic demand; obsolete indicates phase-out. Influences calculation method selection.

attributes.name: demand_variability_coefficient
attributes.description: Statistical measure of demand volatility expressed as coefficient of variation or standard deviation. Used in statistical safety stock calculations to quantify demand uncertainty.

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: The date when this safety stock target becomes active and is used for inventory policy execution, Distribution Requirements Planning (DRP), and Available to Promise (ATP) calculations.

attributes.name: expiration_date
attributes.description: The date when this safety stock target expires and is replaced by a new calculation. Used to manage the lifecycle of safety stock policies and trigger recalculation cycles.

attributes.name: forecast_accuracy_percent
attributes.description: The historical forecast accuracy for this SKU-location combination expressed as a percentage. Used to adjust safety stock calculations based on forecasting reliability. Lower accuracy typically requires higher safety stock.

attributes.name: holding_cost_per_unit
attributes.description: The annual cost to hold one unit of inventory including warehousing, insurance, obsolescence, and capital cost. Used in inventory optimization to balance service level against carrying cost. Expressed in local currency.

attributes.name: is_active
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this safety stock record is currently active and being used for inventory policy execution. True means active; False means superseded or inactive.

attributes.name: last_review_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when the safety stock record was last reviewed by a planner, regardless of whether changes were made. Used to track planner engagement and ensure regular review cycles.

attributes.name: lead_time_variability_days
attributes.description: Measure of supply lead time uncertainty expressed in days. Represents the standard deviation or range of lead time fluctuation used in safety stock calculations.

attributes.name: minimum_order_quantity
attributes.description: The minimum order quantity constraint from the supplier or manufacturing process. Safety stock calculations must consider MOQ to ensure replenishment orders are feasible.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this safety stock record was last modified. Updated whenever any field changes. Supports change tracking and audit requirements.

attributes.name: next_review_date
attributes.description: The scheduled date for the next safety stock review cycle. Used in Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) and Integrated Business Planning (IBP) processes to ensure regular policy updates.

attributes.name: order_multiple
attributes.description: The order quantity increment or lot size multiple required by supplier or production constraints. Safety stock and reorder quantities must align with this multiple.

attributes.name: override_notes
attributes.description: Free-text explanation provided by the planner when manually overriding the calculated safety stock. Documents the business context and rationale for the adjustment.

attributes.name: override_reason_code
attributes.description: The business reason code when approved safety stock differs from calculated safety stock. Captures planner judgment factors such as promotional events, seasonal peaks, supply chain risks, new product introductions, product phase-outs, or quality concerns.

attributes.name: planning_period_end_date
attributes.description: The end date of the planning period for which this safety stock calculation is effective. Defines the validity window for the safety stock target.

attributes.name: planning_period_start_date
attributes.description: The start date of the planning period for which this safety stock calculation is effective. Used in Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) and Integrated Business Planning (IBP) cycles.

attributes.name: record_source
attributes.description: Source system or process that created this record

attributes.name: review_status
attributes.description: The approval workflow status of the safety stock calculation. Pending review indicates awaiting planner action; approved means active for execution; rejected requires recalculation; under revision is being adjusted; expired indicates the planning period has passed.

attributes.name: shelf_life_days
attributes.description: The shelf life or expiration period of the product in days. Critical for Fast-Moving Consumer Goods (FMCG) and Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) safety stock decisions to prevent obsolescence. Influences maximum safety stock levels and First Expired First Out (FEFO) policies.

attributes.name: stockout_cost_per_unit
attributes.description: The estimated cost of a stockout per unit including lost sales, expediting costs, customer dissatisfaction, and brand impact. Used in service level optimization to quantify the cost of Out of Stock (OOS) events. Expressed in local currency.

attributes.name: supply_risk_score
attributes.description: A quantitative risk score assessing supply chain vulnerability for this SKU-location. Higher scores indicate greater risk from supplier reliability, geopolitical factors, transportation disruptions, or single-source dependencies. Used to adjust safety stock buffers.

attributes.name: target_service_level_percent
attributes.description: The desired in-stock probability or fill rate target expressed as a percentage (e.g., 95.00, 98.50). Drives the safety stock buffer size to achieve the specified On Shelf Availability (OSA) or Service Level Agreement (SLA).

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The unit of measure for safety stock quantities. EA=Each, CS=Case, PL=Pallet, KG=Kilogram, LB=Pound, L=Liter, GAL=Gallon. Must align with SKU base unit of measure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PL|KG|LB|L|GAL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: xyz_classification
attributes.description: The XYZ classification based on demand variability. X items have low variability (predictable); Y items have moderate variability; Z items have high variability (unpredictable). Used alongside ABC to determine safety stock strategy.

attributes.name: z_score
attributes.description: The standard normal distribution value corresponding to the target service level. Used in statistical safety stock formulas to translate service level percentage into buffer quantity.

products.name: replenishment_order
products.description: Supply network replenishment order for inter-node stock transfers driven by DRP/MRP planning. SSOT for network-level supply replenishment. Distinct from inventory.inventory_replenishment_order which is location-level restocking.

attributes.name: replenishment_order_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the supply replenishment order record. Primary key for this entity.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Replenishment orders incur expenses that must be tracked against the responsible cost center for expense reporting and internal charge‑backs.

attributes.name: destination_location_supply_network_node_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the supply network node to which inventory will be delivered (distribution center, warehouse, or retail location).

attributes.name: event_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to promotion.promotion_event. Business justification: REQUIRED: Replenishment orders are often generated to satisfy demand generated by a specific promotion event.

attributes.name: network_lane_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.network_lane. Business justification: Each supply replenishment order travels along a specific network lane between source and destination nodes. Linking to network_lane provides access to lane-level attributes (lead time, cost, transport

attributes.name: planned_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to manufacturing.planned_order. Business justification: Before production order conversion, supply replenishment demand is pegged to planned orders. This FK enables pre-conversion demand-supply pegging in IBP, supporting supply exception management and rep

attributes.name: planning_run_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.planning_run. Business justification: Supply replenishment orders are generated as outputs of DRP/MRP planning runs. The existing drp_run_timestamp is a timestamp field, not a proper FK. Adding planning_run_id formalizes the relationship

attributes.name: primary_supply_network_node_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the supply network node from which inventory will be shipped (plant, distribution center, or warehouse).

attributes.name: production_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: Supply replenishment orders for make-to-stock SKUs are fulfilled by production orders. This FK enables demand-supply pegging for ATP calculations and supply chain visibility reporting — a standard IBP

attributes.name: profit_center_id

attributes.name: profit_center_id

attributes.name: transportation_mode

attributes.name: allocated_quantity
attributes.description: The quantity already committed or reserved for existing sales orders, reducing available ATP.

attributes.name: atp_calculation_method
attributes.description: The method used to calculate ATP. Simple ATP considers only on-hand inventory minus allocations. Cumulative ATP includes planned receipts over time. Multi-level ATP considers BOM components. Product allocation ATP applies allocation rules across customer segments.

attributes.name: atp_check_horizon_days
attributes.description: The number of days into the future that the ATP check considers for availability. Defines the planning horizon for promise calculations.

attributes.name: atp_confirmation_number
attributes.description: Unique confirmation number generated when ATP is reserved for a sales order, providing traceability between ATP records and order promising.

attributes.description: The date for which ATP quantity is calculated. Represents the promise date for order fulfillment.

attributes.name: atp_quantity
attributes.description: The confirmed quantity available to promise to customers for the given SKU, location, and date. Represents uncommitted inventory available for new orders.

attributes.name: atp_status
attributes.description: The current status of the ATP record. Available indicates positive ATP. Constrained indicates limited availability. Blocked indicates ATP is not available for promising. Expired indicates the ATP date has passed.

attributes.name: backorder_quantity
attributes.description: The quantity of unfulfilled demand from previous periods that impacts current ATP availability.

attributes.name: calculation_timestamp
attributes.description: The exact date and time when this ATP record was calculated. Critical for understanding data freshness and order promising accuracy.

attributes.name: created_timestamp

attributes.name: cumulative_atp_quantity

attributes.name: demand_pegging_reference

attributes.name: forecast_consumption_quantity

attributes.name: intransit_quantity

attributes.name: minimum_shelf_life_days

attributes.name: on_hand_inventory

attributes.name: planned_receipt_quantity

attributes.name: planning_version

attributes.name: product_allocation_group
attributes.description: The allocation group or priority segment assigned to this ATP calculation, used when ATP is allocated across customer tiers or channels.

attributes.name: production_order_quantity

attributes.name: purchase_order_quantity

attributes.name: safety_stock_quantity
attributes.description: The minimum inventory buffer maintained to protect against demand variability and supply uncertainty. May reduce ATP if policy enforces safety stock protection.

attributes.name: supply_pegging_reference

attributes.name: unit_of_measure

attributes.description: Foreign key linking to procurement.approved_supplier_list. Business justification: SKU planning parameters (lead time, MOQ, procurement type) are governed by the approved supplier list. This FK enables planners to validate planning parameters against current ASL status and supports

attributes.name: inventory_policy_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.inventory_policy. Business justification: SKU planning parameters are governed by inventory policies (service level targets, safety stock methods, replenishment methods). Linking sku_planning_param to inventory_policy formalizes the policy-to

attributes.name: manufacturing_bom_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_bom. Business justification: Supply planners must know which BOM version drives component availability and lead time assumptions in planning parameters. This FK links sku_planning_param to the active manufacturing BOM, enabling B

attributes.name: manufacturing_facility_id
attributes.description: Reference to the manufacturing plant or production facility where this SKU is produced or sourced. Used for plant-specific planning parameters.

attributes.name: packaging_spec_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.product_packaging_spec. Business justification: FEFO inventory planning requires sku_planning_param fields (shelf_life_days, minimum_remaining_shelf_life_days) to be sourced from the authoritative packaging spec. Supply planners configuring FEFO/FI

attributes.name: routing_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to manufacturing.routing. Business justification: Routing defines production lead times and operation sequences that directly populate planned_delivery_time_days and lead_time_days in sku_planning_param. This FK enables automatic lead time updates wh

attributes.name: sku_id
attributes.description: Reference to the SKU in the product master. Links planning parameters to the specific product variant being planned.

attributes.name: standard_cost_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.standard_cost. Business justification: SKU planning parameters include inventory_valuation_method and lot sizing rules that are financially calibrated using standard cost. Consumer goods supply planners use standard cost to set financially

attributes.name: supply_network_node_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.network_node. Business justification: sku_planning_param needs to be associated with the network node it applies to; added new FK supply_network_node_id referencing network_node.supply_network_node_id.

attributes.name: abc_classification
attributes.description: ABC inventory classification based on revenue contribution or consumption value. A items are high-value requiring tight control, C items are low-value with relaxed control.

attributes.name: backorder_allowed_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether backorders are permitted for this SKU when inventory is insufficient. True allows demand to be backordered, false requires immediate fulfillment or cancellation.

attributes.name: coverage_profile_days
attributes.description: Target days of supply to maintain in inventory. Represents desired inventory coverage horizon based on demand forecast and replenishment frequency.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this planning parameter record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.

attributes.name: critical_part_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this SKU is classified as a critical part requiring special planning attention. Critical parts may have higher service levels or expedited replenishment.

attributes.name: data_quality_score
attributes.description: Score indicating the quality of the data record

attributes.name: demand_pattern_type
attributes.description: Characterization of the SKUs historical demand pattern. Determines appropriate forecasting method and safety stock calculation approach.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: Date after which these planning parameters are no longer active. Null indicates parameters are currently active with no planned end date.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: Date from which these planning parameters become active and are used by planning systems. Supports time-phased parameter changes.

attributes.name: fixed_lot_size_quantity
attributes.description: Fixed production or procurement lot size quantity when using fixed lot size method. Represents standard batch size for manufacturing or ordering.

attributes.name: forecast_model_type
attributes.description: Statistical or machine learning model used for demand forecasting. Determines the algorithm applied during forecast generation in IBP or demand planning systems.

attributes.name: goods_receipt_processing_time_days
attributes.description: Time required for quality inspection and goods receipt processing after physical delivery. Adds to total replenishment lead time.

attributes.name: inventory_valuation_method
attributes.description: Method used for inventory valuation and consumption. FIFO (First In First Out), FEFO (First Expired First Out), LIFO (Last In First Out), or weighted average costing.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this planning parameter record was last updated. Tracks most recent change for change management and audit purposes.

attributes.name: last_review_date
attributes.description: Date when planning parameters were last reviewed and validated by the planning team. Supports periodic parameter maintenance and accuracy audits.

attributes.name: lot_size_method
attributes.description: Method used to calculate production or procurement lot sizes. Determines how order quantities are calculated in MRP and supply planning runs.

attributes.name: maximum_order_quantity
attributes.description: Maximum quantity that can be ordered or produced in a single transaction. Limited by production capacity, storage constraints, or supplier capabilities.

attributes.name: maximum_stock_level_quantity
attributes.description: Maximum inventory level target for this SKU. Used in min-max planning to cap inventory investment and prevent overstock situations.

attributes.name: minimum_order_quantity
attributes.description: Minimum quantity that must be ordered or produced in a single transaction. Enforced by supplier contracts or manufacturing constraints.

attributes.name: minimum_remaining_shelf_life_days
attributes.description: Minimum remaining shelf life required at time of shipment to customer or retail delivery. Ensures product quality and retailer acceptance.

attributes.name: mrp_controller_code
attributes.description: Code identifying the MRP controller or planner responsible for this SKU. Used for workload assignment and exception management routing.

attributes.name: mrp_type
attributes.description: MRP procedure that controls how the planning system generates procurement proposals. Determines planning logic and automation level.

attributes.name: next_review_date
attributes.description: Scheduled date for next planning parameter review. Ensures regular validation of planning assumptions and parameter accuracy.

attributes.name: order_multiple_quantity
attributes.description: Quantity increment in which orders must be placed. All order quantities must be multiples of this value due to packaging or production constraints.

attributes.name: parameter_notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes documenting special planning considerations, parameter rationale, or exceptions for this SKU. Provides context for planning decisions.

attributes.name: parameter_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the planning parameter record. Active parameters are used in planning runs, inactive parameters are ignored.

attributes.name: planned_delivery_time_days
attributes.description: Standard lead time in days from order placement to goods receipt. Used for MRP scheduling and supply plan timing calculations.

attributes.name: planning_group
attributes.description: IBP planning group assignment for demand and supply planning segmentation. Groups SKUs with similar planning characteristics for collaborative planning processes.

attributes.name: planning_horizon_days
attributes.description: Number of days into the future for which demand and supply planning is performed. Defines the forward-looking planning window for this SKU.

attributes.name: planning_strategy
attributes.description: Manufacturing planning strategy that determines how the SKU is planned and produced. Make-to-stock produces to forecast, make-to-order produces against customer orders.

attributes.name: planning_time_fence_days
attributes.description: Number of days within which the planning system cannot automatically change planned orders without planner approval. Protects near-term production schedule stability.

attributes.name: procurement_type
attributes.description: Indicates whether the SKU is externally procured from suppliers, produced in-house, or both. Determines supply planning logic and sourcing strategy.

attributes.name: record_source
attributes.description: Source system or process that created this record

attributes.name: reorder_point_quantity
attributes.description: Inventory level that triggers a replenishment order. Calculated based on lead time demand plus safety stock to prevent stockouts.

attributes.name: rounding_profile_code
attributes.description: Code defining rounding rules for planned order quantities. Ensures quantities align with packaging units, pallet configurations, or production batch constraints.

attributes.name: safety_stock_method
attributes.description: Method used to calculate safety stock levels. Fixed quantity uses static buffer, service level based uses demand variability and lead time uncertainty.

attributes.name: seasonality_profile_code
attributes.description: Code identifying the seasonality pattern applied to this SKU for demand forecasting. Links to predefined seasonal index curves (e.g., summer peak, holiday spike).

attributes.name: service_level_target_percent
attributes.description: Target service level percentage for this SKU (e.g., 95%, 98%, 99%). Drives safety stock calculation to achieve desired product availability.

attributes.name: shelf_life_days
attributes.description: Total shelf life of the SKU in days from production date to expiration. Critical for FEFO inventory management and demand planning for perishable goods.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Base unit of measure for all planning quantities (e.g., EA for each, CS for case, KG for kilogram). Standardizes quantity representation across planning processes.

attributes.name: xyz_classification
attributes.description: XYZ demand variability classification. X items have stable demand, Y items have moderate variability, Z items have highly erratic or sporadic demand patterns.

products.name: planning_run
products.description: Master reference table for planning_run. Referenced by planning_run_id.

attributes.name: planning_run_id
attributes.description: Primary key for planning_run

attributes.name: event_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to promotion.promotion_event. Business justification: CPG supply planning runs are frequently triggered by upcoming promotion events (pre-promotion replenishment planning). This FK allows tracing which promotion event triggered a planning run, enabling p

attributes.name: previous_planning_run_id
attributes.description: Self-referencing FK on planning_run (previous_planning_run_id)

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the planning run record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: data_quality_score
attributes.description: Score indicating the quality of the data record

attributes.name: data_source
attributes.description: Origin of the input data used for the planning run.

attributes.name: duration_minutes
attributes.description: Total elapsed time of the run measured in minutes.

attributes.name: error_message
attributes.description: Error details captured when the run fails or encounters issues.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free‑form comments or observations captured by the planner.

attributes.name: outcome_metric
attributes.description: Key quantitative metric produced by the run (e.g., total forecast error, service level).

attributes.name: planning_algorithm
attributes.description: Name or code of the algorithm/model used to generate the plan.

attributes.name: planning_horizon_end
attributes.description: Last date of the planning horizon covered by the run.

attributes.name: planning_horizon_start
attributes.description: First date of the planning horizon covered by the run.

attributes.name: planning_run_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the planning run.

attributes.name: record_source
attributes.description: Source system or process that created this record

attributes.name: result_summary
attributes.description: High‑level textual summary of the planning outcomes.

attributes.name: run_end_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the planning run execution finished.

attributes.name: run_name
attributes.description: Human‑readable name given to the planning run for easy identification.

attributes.name: run_number
attributes.description: External reference number or code used by business users to identify the run.

attributes.name: run_priority
attributes.description: Business‑assigned priority level influencing scheduling and resource allocation.

attributes.name: run_start_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the planning run execution began.

attributes.name: run_type
attributes.description: Category of planning activity performed in the run (e.g., demand, supply, inventory, S&OP, optimization).

attributes.name: scenario_code
attributes.description: Code representing the scenario (e.g., baseline, best‑case, worst‑case) used for this run.

attributes.name: success_flag
attributes.description: True if the run completed without errors; otherwise False.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the planning run record.

attributes.name: version_number
attributes.description: Version identifier for the run, useful for tracking iterative improvements.

domains.name: sales

Manages sales orders, pricing agreements, sales force automation (SFA), retail account call records, POG compliance, RSP/MSRP price management, ACV/TDP/SOM metrics, and field sales KPIs. Integrates with Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud.

products.name: account_assignment
products.description: This association product represents the Role-based assignment between a sales rep and a trade account. It captures which reps are actively (or historically) responsible for a given trade account, in what capacity, and for what period. Each record links one rep to one trade_account with attributes — role, start_date, end_date — that exist only in the context of this assignment relationship and are required for commission calculation and sales performance reporting.. Existence Justification: In consumer goods SFA (Salesforce Automation), a sales rep manages a portfolio of many trade accounts, and a large B2B trade account is actively served by multiple reps in distinct roles (e.g., key account manager, field rep, inside sales rep). This is a well-established operational concept — 'Account Team Membership' or 'Account Assignment' — that businesses actively create, update, and deactivate as territories shift and roles change. The relationship carries its own first-class attributes (role, start_date, end_date) that belong to neither the rep nor the account alone.

attributes.name: end_date
attributes.description: The date on which the rep's assignment to this account in this role ended. Null indicates an active assignment. Required for historical tracking and performance reporting. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.

attributes.name: role
attributes.description: The functional role the rep holds on this account (e.g., Key Account Manager, Field Rep). A single account may have multiple reps in different roles simultaneously. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.
attributes.references: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud Account Team Role

attributes.name: start_date
attributes.description: The date on which the rep's assignment to this account in this role became effective. Required for historical tracking and commission period calculations. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.

domains.description: Owns quality assurance, quality control, and GMP compliance data across the product lifecycle. Manages QC inspection results, non-conformance records, CAPA processes, batch release decisions, stability studies, certificate of analysis, GMP audit findings, supplier quality assessments, product complaints, and regulatory hold events. Integrates with SAP QM and Veeva Vault.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Each distribution facility is a cost center in consumer goods — facility operating costs (labor, utilities, lease, maintenance) are tracked against a cost center for budget control and facility-level

attributes.name: primary_distribution_co_located_manufacturing_facility_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Primary Distribution Co Located Manufacturing Facility Id
attributes.description: FK to manufacturing facility if co-located

tag_set.value: Primary Distribution Co Located Manufacturing Facility Id

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Distribution facilities in multi-brand consumer goods companies are mapped to profit centers for regional/channel P&L reporting. Facility throughput and logistics costs are attributed to a profit cent

attributes.name: supply_network_node_id
attributes.description: Links DC to supply network node; distinct from manufacturing_facility which is a production site

attributes.name: address_line_1
attributes.description: Primary street address line for the distribution center facility. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.

attributes.name: address_line_2
attributes.description: Secondary address line for suite, building, or unit information. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.
