attributes.description: The date when inventory was last moved into or out of this storage location. Used for slow-moving inventory identification.

attributes.name: last_movement_type
attributes.description: The type of the last inventory movement transaction that affected this position (receipt, putaway, pick, replenishment, transfer, adjustment, return). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: receipt|putaway|pick|replenishment|transfer|adjustment|return — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: last_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this inventory position record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data freshness assessment.

attributes.name: license_plate_number
attributes.description: The warehouse management system license plate number assigned to the handling unit. Used for tracking and automated material handling.

attributes.name: lot_number
attributes.description: The alphanumeric lot or batch number assigned during manufacturing. Used for traceability and recall management.

attributes.name: manufacture_date
attributes.description: The date on which the product lot was manufactured. Used for shelf-life calculations and FEFO rotation.

attributes.name: owner_type
attributes.description: Indicates the ownership model of the inventory (owned by company, consignment from supplier, customer-owned for returns, vendor-managed inventory).

attributes.name: pallet_code
attributes.description: The unique identifier of the pallet or handling unit on which the inventory is stored. Used for warehouse automation and tracking.

attributes.name: pick_face_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this inventory position is in a primary pick face location (true) or reserve storage (false). Pick face locations are optimized for order picking efficiency.

attributes.name: putaway_date
attributes.description: The date when the inventory was put away into this storage location after receiving. Used for aging analysis and FEFO compliance.

attributes.name: quantity_allocated
attributes.description: The quantity of on-hand inventory that has been allocated to outbound orders or reservations but not yet picked. Reduces available-to-pick quantity.

attributes.name: quantity_available
attributes.description: The quantity available for new order allocation and picking. Calculated as quantity_on_hand minus quantity_allocated minus quantity_quarantine minus quantity_hold.

attributes.name: quantity_damaged
attributes.description: The quantity of inventory identified as damaged and not suitable for sale or distribution. Typically awaiting disposal or return to supplier.

attributes.name: quantity_hold
attributes.description: The quantity of inventory placed on hold due to quality issues, customer complaints, or pending investigation. Not available for picking.

attributes.name: quantity_on_hand
attributes.description: The total physical quantity of the SKU-lot currently present in the storage location. Measured in the SKU's base unit of measure.

attributes.name: quantity_quarantine
attributes.description: The quantity of inventory placed in quarantine status pending quality inspection or regulatory clearance. Not available for picking.

attributes.name: quantity_reserved
attributes.description: The quantity of inventory reserved for specific customers, channels, or promotional programs. Subset of allocated quantity with additional business constraints.

attributes.name: receipt_date
attributes.description: The date when the inventory lot was received into the distribution center. Used for inventory aging and supplier performance tracking.

attributes.name: replenishment_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this inventory position requires replenishment from reserve to pick face (true) or not (false). Triggers automated replenishment tasks.

attributes.name: snapshot_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this inventory position snapshot was captured. Used for point-in-time inventory reporting and trend analysis.

attributes.name: storage_zone
attributes.description: The logical zone within the distribution center where the inventory is stored (e.g., ambient, refrigerated, frozen, hazmat, high-velocity, reserve).

attributes.name: temperature_zone
attributes.description: The temperature control zone classification for the storage location. Critical for cold chain compliance and product quality.

attributes.name: total_inventory_value
attributes.description: The total financial value of the inventory position calculated as quantity_on_hand multiplied by cost_per_unit. Used for balance sheet reporting. Business confidential financial data.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The base unit of measure in which inventory quantities are tracked (Each, Case, Pallet, Pound, Kilogram, Liter, Gallon). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PL|LB|KG|L|GAL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: weight_unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The unit of measure for actual weight (Pound, Kilogram, Ounce, Gram). Applicable for catch weight items.

domains.name: procurement
domains.description: Owns sourcing, supplier management, and purchase order execution for raw materials, packaging, and indirect goods. Manages supplier master data, contracts, RFQs, purchase requisitions, PO lifecycle, goods receipt, invoice verification, MOQ terms, SDS documentation, supplier performance scorecards, VMI programs, and sustainable sourcing initiatives (FSC, RSPO).

domains.name: promotion
domains.description: Owns trade promotion planning, execution, and optimization (TPM/TPO). Manages promotional calendars, trade spend allocation, retailer funding agreements, promotional pricing (Hi-Lo, EDLP), accruals, deductions, post-event analysis, promotional lift measurement, ROI/GMROI analysis, and retailer rebate settlements. Integrates with Salesforce TPM.

products.name: trade_promotion
products.description: Core master entity for trade promotion programs. Captures the full definition of a trade promotion including name, type (Hi-Lo, EDLP, display, coupon, scan-back, off-invoice), promotional period, owning brand/category, status (planned, active, closed, cancelled), total authorized trade spend budget, promotion objectives, promotion type classification (temporary price reduction, feature ad, display, BOGO, multi-buy, scan-back, coupon, loyalty reward), applicable channels, and integration reference to Salesforce TPM. Serves as the SSOT for all trade promotion definitions and the parent entity for all promotion events, spend allocations, and post-event analyses.
products.reference: TPM Promotion Header (Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud)

attributes.name: trade_promotion_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the trade promotion program. Primary key for the trade promotion entity.

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.product_brand. Business justification: Trade promotions in consumer goods are planned and budgeted at brand level (e.g., 'Dove Q3 FSI campaign'). Brand-level trade spend reporting, ROI analysis, and TPM planning all require direct brand li

attributes.name: company_code_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Multi-entity CPG companies execute trade promotions within specific legal entities. Company code drives currency, fiscal year variant, and statutory reporting. A CPG finance expert expects every trade

attributes.name: price_list_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.price_list. Business justification: Promotional Pricing Setup: trade promotions are built as discounts off a base price list. TPM planners must reference the standard price list to compute discount depth, validate promotional price floo

attributes.name: product_category_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the product category covered by this trade promotion. Links to the product category master entity.

attributes.name: rep_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.rep. Business justification: Promotion Ownership: in consumer goods field sales, each trade promotion is owned by a key account manager or sales rep for accountability, commission calculation, and rep performance reporting. TPM s

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: REQUIRED: Supplier‑funded trade promotions need a link for budgeting and compliance; experts expect a sponsor supplier on each promotion.

attributes.name: territory_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Promotion Territory Scoping: trade promotions in consumer goods are targeted to specific sales territories for geographic execution planning and rep accountability. geographic_scope is a denormalized

attributes.name: trade_account_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the retail trade account (customer) participating in this promotion. Links to the trade account master entity.

attributes.name: trade_calendar_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to promotion.trade_calendar. Business justification: A trade promotion program is planned and executed within a specific trade calendar period. This is a foundational TPM relationship: promotions are organized under annual or periodic trade calendars fo

attributes.name: accrual_amount
attributes.description: Total accrued trade spend liability for this promotion, representing funds reserved or committed but not yet paid to the retailer.

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date when the trade promotion was formally approved by authorized business stakeholders and authorized for execution.

attributes.name: authorized_budget_amount
attributes.description: Total authorized trade spend budget allocated for this promotion, including all funding mechanisms (off-invoice, scan-back, display allowances, etc.). Expressed in the company's reporting currency.

attributes.name: baseline_volume_units
attributes.description: Expected sales volume in units without the promotion, used as the baseline for calculating incremental lift and ROI.

attributes.name: channel_type
attributes.description: Retail channel classification where this promotion is executed. Grocery (supermarkets), mass merchandiser (Walmart/Target), drug (pharmacy chains), convenience (c-stores), club (warehouse clubs), ecommerce (online retail).

attributes.name: country_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where this promotion is executed.

attributes.name: coupon_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this promotion includes manufacturer or retailer coupons (print, digital, or mobile). True if coupons are part of the promotion, false otherwise.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this trade promotion record was first created in the system. Represents the business event time of promotion planning initiation.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this promotion record.

attributes.name: deduction_amount
attributes.description: Total deductions taken by the retailer against invoices for this promotion. Used for reconciliation and settlement tracking.

attributes.name: discount_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage discount offered to the retailer or consumer as part of this promotion. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 15.00 for 15% off).

attributes.name: display_type
attributes.description: Type of in-store merchandising display associated with this promotion. End-cap (end of aisle), free-standing (floor display), shelf-talker (shelf signage), pallet (bulk display), cooler (refrigerated display), none (no special display).

attributes.name: end_date
attributes.description: The date when the trade promotion concludes and promotional pricing/terms expire at retail.

attributes.name: expected_roi_percentage
attributes.description: Expected return on investment for this promotion, calculated as (incremental profit / trade spend) * 100. Used for pre-event planning and approval.

attributes.name: feature_ad_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this promotion includes feature advertising in retailer circulars, flyers, or digital media. True if featured, false otherwise.

attributes.name: funding_type
attributes.description: Mechanism by which trade funds are provided to the retailer. Off-invoice (deducted at purchase), scan-back (reimbursed based on POS sales), bill-back (invoiced after event), lump-sum (fixed payment), accrual (reserved funds).

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this trade promotion record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trail purposes.

attributes.name: maximum_purchase_quantity
attributes.description: Maximum number of units a consumer can purchase at the promotional price. Null if no maximum limit applies.

attributes.name: minimum_purchase_quantity
attributes.description: Minimum number of units a consumer must purchase to qualify for the promotional offer. Null if no minimum applies.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional business context, special terms, execution notes, or post-event observations related to this trade promotion.

attributes.name: pricing_strategy
attributes.description: Retailer pricing strategy context for this promotion. Hi-Lo (high-low promotional pricing with deep discounts), EDLP (everyday low price with minimal promotions), hybrid (combination approach).

attributes.name: promotion_name
attributes.description: Descriptive name of the trade promotion program for business user identification and reporting purposes.

attributes.name: promotion_number
attributes.description: Business-facing unique identifier for the trade promotion program, typically generated by the TPM system. Format: TPM-YYYYNNNN where YYYY is year and NNNN is sequence.

attributes.name: promotion_objective
attributes.description: Primary business objective driving the trade promotion strategy. Volume growth (increase unit sales), market share gain (capture competitor share), new product launch (trial generation), competitive defense (respond to competitor activity), inventory clearance (reduce excess stock), seasonal event (holiday/event-driven).

attributes.name: promotion_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the trade promotion. Planned (in design), approved (authorized for execution), active (currently running), closed (completed with results finalized), cancelled (terminated before completion), on-hold (temporarily suspended).

attributes.name: promotion_type
attributes.description: Classification of the trade promotion mechanism. Temporary price reduction (Hi-Lo), feature ad (retailer circular), display (end-cap/shelf), BOGO (buy-one-get-one), multi-buy (quantity discount), scan-back (post-sale rebate). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: temporary_price_reduction|feature_ad|display|bogo|multi_buy|scan_back|off_invoice|coupon|loyalty_reward|edlp|slotting_allowance|markdown_allowance — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: settlement_status
attributes.description: Status of financial settlement and reconciliation for trade promotion deductions and accruals. Pending (awaiting settlement), in-progress (reconciliation underway), settled (fully reconciled and paid), disputed (discrepancies under review).

attributes.name: start_date
attributes.description: The date when the trade promotion becomes active and promotional pricing/terms take effect at retail.

attributes.name: target_volume_units
attributes.description: Target sales volume in units expected to be achieved during the promotion period, including baseline and incremental lift.

attributes.name: tpm_system_reference_code
attributes.business_glossary_term: Trade Promotion Management (TPM) System Reference ID
attributes.description: External reference identifier from the source Trade Promotion Management system (e.g., Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud TPM module). Used for system integration and traceability.

tag_set.value: Trade Promotion Management (TPM) System Reference ID

products.name: event
products.description: Trade promotion event representing a specific promotional execution with a retailer (TPR, display, feature). SSOT for trade promotion events. Distinct from marketing.marketing_event which covers brand marketing events.

attributes.name: event_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the promotion event. Primary key for this entity.

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.product_brand. Business justification: Post-event analysis and brand-level promotional ROI reporting are standard consumer goods TPM processes. Promotion events are executed at brand level (e.g., brand display programs). Direct brand FK en

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for promotion spend accounting reports linking each event to the responsible cost center for budgeting and variance analysis.

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Enables profit‑center level reporting of promotion performance and ROI, a standard finance requirement.

attributes.name: rep_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.rep. Business justification: Event Execution Ownership: promotion events are executed by field sales reps responsible for in-store compliance, display setup, and post-event analysis sign-off. Linking promotion_event to rep enable

attributes.name: retail_store_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific retail store, distribution center, or ship-to location where the promotional event is executed.

attributes.name: trade_account_id
attributes.description: Reference to the retailer or distributor account participating in this promotional event.

attributes.name: trade_calendar_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to promotion.trade_calendar. Business justification: A promotion event (specific retailer execution) is scheduled within a trade calendar period. This link enables calendar-based reporting of event activity, planned vs. actual event counts (trade_calend

attributes.name: trade_promotion_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent trade promotion program under which this event is executed. A single program may have multiple events across different retailers or time windows.

attributes.name: accrual_amount
attributes.description: The financial accrual amount reserved for this promotional event, representing the estimated liability for trade promotion obligations.

attributes.name: actual_trade_spend_amount
attributes.description: The realized trade spend for this promotional event, representing the total investment actually incurred by the manufacturer.

attributes.name: actual_volume_units
attributes.description: The realized sales volume in units achieved during the promotional event, used for post-event analysis and lift measurement.

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: The date when this promotional event was approved for execution by the authorized business stakeholder.

attributes.name: baseline_volume_units
attributes.description: The expected sales volume in units without promotional activity, used as the baseline for calculating promotional lift.

attributes.name: cancellation_date
attributes.description: The date when this promotional event was cancelled, if applicable.

attributes.name: cancellation_reason
attributes.description: Textual explanation for why this promotional event was cancelled, if applicable.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this promotional event record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this promotional event record.

attributes.name: deduction_amount
attributes.description: The total deductions claimed by the retailer against invoices for this promotional event, representing retailer-initiated trade spend recovery.

attributes.name: description
attributes.description: Detailed textual description of the promotional event, including objectives, mechanics, and special conditions.

attributes.name: end_date
attributes.description: The date when the promotional event concludes execution at retail or distribution level.

attributes.name: event_number
attributes.description: Business-facing unique identifier for the promotion event, used for external communication and reporting.

attributes.name: event_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the promotional event. Planned: event scheduled but not yet approved; Approved: event approved for execution; In Execution: event currently active; Completed: event finished; Cancelled: event terminated before completion.

attributes.name: event_type
attributes.description: Classification of the promotional event mechanism. Feature Ad: product featured in retailer circular; Display: in-store display placement; Temporary Price Reduction (TPR): short-term price discount; BOGO: Buy One Get One offer; Scan-Back: post-sale rebate based on scanned sales; Coupon: manufacturer or retailer coupon redemption.

attributes.name: funding_source
attributes.description: The primary source of funding for this promotional event. Manufacturer: fully funded by manufacturer; Retailer: fully funded by retailer; Cooperative: jointly funded by both parties.

attributes.name: geography_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO country code representing the geographic market where the event is executed.

attributes.name: gmroi_ratio
attributes.business_glossary_term: Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI) Ratio
attributes.description: The gross margin return on investment for this promotional event, calculated as Gross Margin / Average Inventory Investment, measuring profitability efficiency.

tag_set.value: Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI) Ratio

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this promotional event record was last modified in the system.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Descriptive name of the promotional event for business user identification and reporting.

attributes.name: planned_trade_spend_amount
attributes.description: The budgeted trade spend allocation for this promotional event, representing the total investment planned by the manufacturer.

attributes.name: planned_volume_units
attributes.description: The forecasted sales volume in units expected during the promotional event, used for planning and ROI estimation.

attributes.name: post_event_analysis_completed_flag
attributes.description: Boolean indicator of whether post-event analysis has been completed for this promotional event, including lift measurement and ROI calculation.

attributes.name: post_event_analysis_date
attributes.description: The date when post-event analysis was completed for this promotional event.

attributes.name: pricing_strategy
attributes.description: The pricing approach applied during this event. Hi-Lo: high-low promotional pricing with deep temporary discounts; EDLP: Everyday Low Price with minimal promotional variation; Hybrid: combination of both strategies.

attributes.name: promotional_lift_percentage
attributes.description: The percentage increase in sales volume attributable to the promotional event, calculated as (Actual Volume - Baseline Volume) / Baseline Volume * 100.

attributes.name: rebate_amount
attributes.description: The total rebate amount paid to the retailer for this promotional event, typically calculated post-event based on performance metrics.

attributes.name: roi_percentage
attributes.description: The return on investment for this promotional event, calculated as (Incremental Profit - Trade Spend) / Trade Spend * 100, measuring promotional effectiveness.

attributes.name: salesforce_tpm_event_code
attributes.business_glossary_term: Salesforce Trade Promotion Management (TPM) Event ID
attributes.description: The unique identifier from Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud Trade Promotion Management system for this event record, used for system integration and data lineage.

tag_set.value: Salesforce Trade Promotion Management (TPM) Event ID

attributes.name: settlement_date
attributes.description: The date when financial settlement for this promotional event was completed and all trade spend obligations were resolved.

attributes.name: settlement_status
attributes.description: The current status of financial settlement for this promotional event. Pending: settlement not yet initiated; In Progress: settlement calculations underway; Settled: all financial obligations resolved; Disputed: settlement under dispute resolution.

attributes.name: start_date
attributes.description: The date when the promotional event begins execution at retail or distribution level.

products.name: event_sku
products.description: Association entity linking individual SKUs (GTINs/UPCs) to a specific promotion event with full promotional pricing authority. Captures SKU-level promotional details including promoted price, promoted price type (Hi-Lo temporary price reduction, EDLP, multi-buy, BOGO, scan-back, off-invoice, bill-back), price reduction depth (%), regular shelf price (RSP/MSRP), effective date range, planned promotional volume (cases/units), actual promotional volume, baseline volume estimate, incremental lift volume, promotional discount amount per unit, retailer account, channel, and pricing approval status. Serves as the SSOT for SKU-level promotional pricing within trade events — distinct from standard list pricing owned by the product domain. Enables SKU-level trade spend, lift analysis, and promotional price compliance per event.
products.reference: TPM Event Product Line (Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud)

attributes.name: event_sku_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Promotion Event SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) ID
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the promotion event SKU association record. Primary key for this entity.

tag_set.value: Promotion Event SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) ID

attributes.name: event_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent promotion event to which this SKU is associated.

attributes.name: price_list_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.price_list_item. Business justification: Price Reduction Depth Compliance: each event SKU's promotional discount is measured against the standard price_list_item unit_price. This FK enables 'Promotional Lift vs. List Price' compliance verifi

attributes.name: retailer_funding_agreement_id
attributes.description: Reference to the retailer funding agreement or trade contract governing the promotional terms for this SKU.

attributes.name: sku_id
attributes.description: Reference to the product SKU participating in this promotion event.

attributes.name: trade_account_id
attributes.description: Reference to the retailer or trade account for which this promotional SKU pricing applies.

attributes.name: accrual_amount
attributes.description: Accrued trade spend liability for this promotional SKU. Represents the estimated financial obligation based on planned volume and discount.

attributes.name: actual_promotional_volume_cases
attributes.description: Actual volume of cases sold during the promotion period for this SKU. Captured post-event from POS or shipment data.

attributes.name: actual_promotional_volume_units
attributes.description: Actual volume of consumer units sold during the promotion period for this SKU. Used for promotional lift and ROI analysis.

attributes.name: baseline_volume_estimate_units
attributes.description: Estimated volume of units that would have been sold without the promotion. Used to calculate incremental lift. Typically derived from historical sales trends.

attributes.name: compliance_check_status
attributes.description: Status of promotional pricing compliance verification for this SKU. Ensures promotional pricing was executed as agreed with the retailer.

attributes.name: compliance_verified_date
attributes.description: Date when promotional pricing compliance was verified for this SKU through retail execution audits or POS data validation.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this promotion event SKU record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: deduction_amount
attributes.description: Actual deduction amount claimed by the retailer for this promotional SKU. Used for trade spend reconciliation and settlement.

attributes.name: display_location_type
attributes.description: Type of in-store display location where this promotional SKU is merchandised. Impacts visibility and promotional lift. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Shelf|End Cap|Free Standing Display|Pallet|Cooler|Checkout|Secondary Placement — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: feature_type
attributes.description: Type of promotional feature or advertising support provided for this SKU during the promotion event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Circular Ad|Digital Ad|In-Store Signage|Shelf Talker|Coupon|Loyalty Offer|None — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: gtin
attributes.description: Global Trade Item Number uniquely identifying the product SKU in the promotion. Supports EAN-8, EAN-13, UPC-A, UPC-E, and GTIN-14 formats.

attributes.name: incremental_lift_percent
attributes.description: Percentage lift in volume due to the promotion. Calculated as (incremental_lift_volume_units / baseline_volume_estimate_units) * 100.

attributes.name: incremental_lift_volume_units
attributes.description: Incremental volume of units sold above baseline due to the promotion. Calculated as actual_promotional_volume_units - baseline_volume_estimate_units.

attributes.name: is_featured_sku
attributes.description: Indicates whether this SKU is a featured hero product in the promotion event. Featured SKUs typically receive premium merchandising and advertising support.

attributes.name: modified_by
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the user who last modified this promotion event SKU record.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this promotion event SKU record was last modified.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-form text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this promotional SKU.

attributes.name: planned_promotional_volume_cases
attributes.description: Forecasted volume of cases expected to be sold during the promotion period for this SKU. Used for trade spend budgeting and supply planning.

attributes.name: planned_promotional_volume_units
attributes.description: Forecasted volume of consumer units expected to be sold during the promotion period for this SKU.

attributes.name: price_reduction_depth_percent
attributes.description: Percentage discount from regular shelf price to promoted price. Calculated as ((regular_shelf_price - promoted_price) / regular_shelf_price) * 100.

attributes.name: pricing_approval_status
attributes.description: Current approval status of the promotional pricing for this SKU. Tracks the workflow state from draft through execution to completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Draft|Pending Approval|Approved|Rejected|Active|Completed|Cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: pricing_approved_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the promotional pricing for this SKU was approved.

attributes.name: promoted_price
attributes.description: The temporary promotional price offered to consumers during the promotion event. Expressed in the base currency of the business.

attributes.name: promoted_price_type
attributes.description: Type of promotional pricing mechanism applied to this SKU. Hi-Lo: temporary price reduction; EDLP: everyday low price; Multi-Buy: quantity discount; BOGO: buy-one-get-one; Scan-Back: retailer scanned rebate; Off-Invoice: deduction at invoice; Bill-Back: post-event settlement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Hi-Lo|EDLP|Multi-Buy|BOGO|Scan-Back|Off-Invoice|Bill-Back — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: promotion_effective_end_date
attributes.description: The date when the promotional pricing for this SKU expires and regular pricing resumes.

attributes.name: promotion_effective_start_date
attributes.description: The date when the promotional pricing for this SKU becomes effective at retail.

attributes.name: promotional_discount_per_unit
attributes.description: Absolute discount amount per unit (regular_shelf_price - promoted_price). Represents the per-unit trade spend investment.

attributes.name: promotional_gmroi
attributes.business_glossary_term: Promotional GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment)
attributes.description: Gross margin return on investment for this promotional SKU. Calculated as incremental_gross_margin / total_trade_spend_amount.

tag_set.value: Promotional GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment)

attributes.name: promotional_roi_percent
attributes.description: Return on investment for this promotional SKU. Calculated as (incremental_gross_profit - total_trade_spend_amount) / total_trade_spend_amount * 100.

attributes.name: rebate_amount
attributes.description: Rebate amount paid to the retailer for this promotional SKU. Typically settled post-event based on actual sales performance.

attributes.name: settlement_date
attributes.description: Date when the financial settlement for this promotional SKU was completed with the retailer.

attributes.name: settlement_status
attributes.description: Current status of the financial settlement process for this promotional SKU with the retailer.

attributes.name: total_trade_spend_amount
attributes.description: Total trade spend investment for this SKU in this promotion event. Calculated as promotional_discount_per_unit * actual_promotional_volume_units.

attributes.name: upc
attributes.description: Universal Product Code for the SKU participating in the promotion event. Standard 12-digit UPC-A format.

products.name: trade_calendar
products.description: Master entity representing the annual or periodic trade promotional calendar for a brand, category, or retailer account. Captures calendar name, fiscal year, planning cycle, owning brand/category manager, retailer account scope, calendar status (draft, approved, locked), total planned trade spend envelope, and number of planned promotion events. Serves as the planning container for all trade promotion events within a given period and account relationship.

attributes.name: trade_calendar_id
attributes.description: Primary key for trade_calendar

attributes.name: brand_id

attributes.description: Identifier of the parent promotional calendar if this is a sub-calendar or regional variant. Links to another promotional calendar entity.

attributes.name: product_category_id

attributes.name: trade_account_id

attributes.name: calendar_name
attributes.description: Business name of the promotional calendar (e.g., 'FY2024 Q1 Retail Promo Calendar', 'Annual Brand X Trade Plan').

attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the promotional calendar (draft, submitted, approved, locked, active, closed).

attributes.name: country_code

attributes.name: fiscal_year

attributes.description: The date when this promotional calendar was locked to prevent further changes, typically after approval and before execution.

attributes.description: Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this promotional calendar for internal reference.

attributes.name: planned_event_count
attributes.description: The total number of promotional events planned within this calendar period.

attributes.name: planning_cycle
attributes.description: The planning frequency or cycle for this promotional calendar (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, monthly).

attributes.name: pricing_strategy
attributes.description: The primary pricing strategy employed in this promotional calendar: Hi-Lo (High-Low Pricing Strategy), EDLP (Everyday Low Price), hybrid, promotional, or premium.

attributes.name: version_number

products.name: trade_spend_allocation

Tracks allocated amount, spend type (off-invoice discount, scan-back, bill-back, lump sum, co-op advertising, slotting fee, display allowance, free goods), spend category classification, GL account mapping, fiscal period, cost center, approval status, and variance between planned and committed spend. Feeds into COGS and trade spend reporting for S&OP and finance reconciliation.

attributes.name: funding_agreement_id

Linking trade_spend_allocation to funding_agreement enables tracking of how committed funding agreement amoun

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Trade spend allocations are assigned to profit centers for brand and channel P&L reporting. profit_center_code is a denormalized plain attribute. CPG finance teams require profit center assignment on

attributes.name: trade_account_id
attributes.description: Reference to the retailer or distributor account receiving the trade spend funding. Identifies the counterparty in the promotional agreement.

attributes.name: accrual_amount
attributes.description: The amount accrued for this trade spend allocation in the financial period. Used for matching expenses to the period in which the promotional activity occurs, per accrual accounting principles.

attributes.name: actual_amount

attributes.description: The monetary amount of trade spend allocated to this promotion event, account, brand, or SKU group. Represents the planned or budgeted spend.

products.name: funding_agreement

Serves as the contractual basis for trade spend commitments and deduction settlements.

attributes.name: funding_agreement_id

attributes.description: Reference to the retail trade account (customer) that is party to this funding agreement.

attributes.name: agreement_number

attributes.name: agreement_status

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|active|suspended|terminated|expired — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: agreement_type

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Current approval workflow status: not submitted (draft), pending review (awaiting decision), approved (authorized), rejected (denied), or revision required (needs changes before resubmission).

attributes.name: approved_by

attributes.name: approved_date

attributes.name: auto_renewal_flag

attributes.name: currency_code

attributes.name: deduction_settlement_terms

attributes.name: funding_period_start_date

attributes.name: gmroi_target

attributes.name: manufacturer_signature_date
attributes.description: Date when the manufacturer signatory signed the funding agreement.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-form text field for additional notes, comments, or special conditions related to the funding agreement.

attributes.name: paid_to_date_amount
attributes.description: Cumulative amount of funding actually paid out to the retailer under this agreement as of the current date.

attributes.name: payment_frequency
attributes.description: Frequency at which payments are made under this agreement: one-time (lump sum), monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, annual, or event-based (triggered by specific promotional events).

attributes.name: payment_terms
attributes.description: Detailed description of payment terms, including timing, method, and conditions for fund disbursement (e.g., quarterly installments, post-event settlement, net 30 days).

attributes.name: performance_conditions
attributes.description: Detailed description of performance metrics, targets, or conditions that must be met for funding to be released or for rebates to be earned (e.g., minimum sales volume, market share targets, display compliance).

attributes.name: remaining_balance_amount
attributes.description: Remaining uncommitted or unpaid funding balance available under this agreement (total committed minus paid to date).

attributes.name: renewal_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this funding agreement is a renewal of a previous agreement (true) or a new agreement (false).

attributes.name: retailer_signatory_name
attributes.description: Full name of the retailer representative who signed the funding agreement on behalf of the retail account.

attributes.name: retailer_signatory_title
attributes.description: Job title or role of the retailer signatory (e.g., Category Manager, Buyer, VP of Merchandising).

attributes.name: retailer_signature_date
attributes.description: Date when the retailer signatory signed the funding agreement.

attributes.name: roi_target_percentage
attributes.description: Target return on investment percentage for this funding agreement, used to measure promotional effectiveness and justify trade spend allocation.

attributes.name: termination_date
attributes.description: Actual date when the agreement was terminated early, if applicable. Null if the agreement was not terminated.

attributes.name: termination_notice_days
attributes.description: Number of days advance notice required by either party to terminate the agreement prior to the end date.

attributes.name: termination_reason
attributes.description: Explanation or reason code for early termination of the agreement (e.g., breach of contract, mutual agreement, business restructuring, poor performance).

attributes.name: total_committed_amount
attributes.description: Total funding amount committed by the manufacturer to the retailer under this agreement, in the agreement currency.

attributes.name: event_id
attributes.description: Reference to the associated trade promotion event for which this accrual is recognized.

Business justification: Promotion accruals represent expected spend obligations that are often tied to specific funding agreements. Linking promotion_accrual to funding_agreement enables reconciliation of accrued amounts aga

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Accrual posting needs a GL account reference; replaces denormalized gl_account_code for proper ledger integration.

attributes.name: journal_entry_id

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Promotion accruals are assigned to profit centers for brand P&L reporting. profit_center_code is a denormalized plain attribute. CPG finance requires profit center on accruals to correctly attribute t

attributes.name: sku_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific product SKU included in the promotional accrual. Nullable for lump-sum accruals not tied to specific SKUs.

attributes.name: trade_account_id
attributes.description: Reference to the retailer or customer account receiving the promotional funding.

attributes.name: accrual_number
attributes.description: Business-readable unique identifier for the accrual record, used for tracking and reconciliation.

attributes.name: accrual_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the accrual: open (recognized but not yet settled), pending approval, approved, settled (paid out), reversed (accounting adjustment), or cancelled.

attributes.name: accrual_type
attributes.description: Classification of the promotional accrual mechanism: scan-back (retailer submits proof of sale), bill-back (post-event invoice deduction), off-invoice (immediate discount), lump-sum (fixed payment), volume rebate, or performance bonus.

attributes.name: amount
attributes.description: Total monetary value of the promotional liability recognized in the accrual, expressed in the functional currency.

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Approval workflow status for the accrual: pending (awaiting review), approved (authorized for settlement), or rejected (denied).

attributes.name: approval_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the accrual was approved. Nullable for unapproved accruals.

attributes.name: baseline_volume
attributes.description: Expected sales volume without the promotion, used to calculate incremental lift and accrual accuracy. Nullable for non-lift-based accruals.

attributes.name: context
attributes.description: Promotion-specific accrual context

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the accrual record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the accrual amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

attributes.name: deduction_claim_number
attributes.description: Reference number for the retailer deduction claim associated with this accrual, used for deduction management and reconciliation. Nullable if no claim submitted.

attributes.name: dispute_reason
attributes.description: Explanation of why the accrual is disputed. Nullable if not disputed.

attributes.name: fiscal_period
attributes.description: Fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year in which the accrual is recognized.

attributes.name: fiscal_year

attributes.name: gmroi
attributes.description: Gross margin return on investment for the promotional spend, measuring profitability efficiency. Nullable if not yet calculated.

attributes.name: incremental_volume
attributes.description: Additional sales volume generated by the promotion above the baseline, used for ROI and GMROI analysis. Nullable for non-lift-based accruals.

attributes.name: is_disputed
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether the accrual is under dispute with the retailer or customer (true if disputed, false otherwise).

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the accrual record was last modified.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional comments, explanations, or special instructions related to the accrual.

attributes.name: payment_reference_number
attributes.description: Reference number for the payment transaction that settled this accrual. Nullable for unsettled accruals.

attributes.name: period_end_date
attributes.description: End date of the accounting period during which the promotional liability is recognized.

attributes.name: period_start_date
attributes.description: Start date of the accounting period during which the promotional liability is recognized.

attributes.name: quantity_sold
attributes.description: Total quantity of product units sold during the promotional period that triggered the accrual. Nullable for non-volume-based accruals.

attributes.name: rate
attributes.description: Rate or percentage applied to calculate the accrual amount (e.g., discount percentage, rebate rate per unit). Nullable for lump-sum accruals.

attributes.name: recognition_date
attributes.description: Date on which the accrual was initially recognized in the financial system.

attributes.name: reversal_date
attributes.description: Date on which the accrual was reversed due to adjustment or cancellation. Nullable for non-reversed accruals.

attributes.name: roi_percentage
attributes.description: Calculated return on investment for the promotional spend, expressed as a percentage. Nullable if not yet calculated.

attributes.name: sap_co_document_number
attributes.description: SAP CO document number for the controlling posting of the accrual, used for cost allocation and internal reporting.

attributes.name: sap_fi_document_number
attributes.description: SAP FI document number for the financial posting of the accrual, used for audit trail and reconciliation.

attributes.name: settlement_date
attributes.description: Date on which the accrual was settled (paid out to the retailer or customer). Nullable for unsettled accruals.

attributes.name: source_system_code
attributes.description: Unique identifier of the accrual record in the source system, used for data lineage and reconciliation.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the quantity sold (e.g., each, case, pallet, kilogram, liter).

products.name: deduction
products.description: Transactional record representing the full lifecycle of a retailer deduction from initial short-payment through final settlement. Captures deduction number, retailer account, invoice reference, deduction amount, deduction type (promotional, pricing, shortage, compliance), deduction date, dispute status (open, approved, disputed, written-off), and complete settlement details: settlement date, settlement method (credit memo, check, offset against future invoice, write-off), settled amount, approved amount, disputed amount, settlement reason code, approver, and linkage to originating promotion event and funding agreement. Serves as the single entity for the entire deduction management workflow — no separate settlement record exists. Central to trade spend reconciliation, dispute resolution, and retailer relationship management.

attributes.name: deduction_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the promotion deduction record. Primary key for the promotion deduction entity representing the complete lifecycle from initial short-payment through final settlement.

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.product_brand. Business justification: Brand-level deduction management and dispute resolution are standard consumer goods AR/TPM processes. Brand teams own deduction liability; brand-level aging and dispute reports require direct brand FK

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Links each deduction to the cost center responsible for the expense, required for cost allocation and reporting.

attributes.name: event_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the originating promotion event that triggered this deduction. Links to the promotion event master.

attributes.name: funding_agreement_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the trade promotion funding agreement under which this deduction was claimed. Links to the funding agreement master.

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Deduction entries must be posted to a specific GL account; removes redundant gl_account_code column.

attributes.name: invoice_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the invoice against which the deduction was taken. Links to the originating invoice record.

attributes.name: journal_entry_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Promotion deductions generate journal entries for AR clearing and deduction write-off in CPG. Linking deduction to journal_entry enables end-to-end audit trail from retailer deduction claim to GL post

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Provides profit‑center level visibility of deduction impact, supporting profit analysis and KPI tracking.

attributes.name: trade_account_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the retailer or trade account that initiated the deduction. Links to the customer trade account master.

attributes.name: accrual_impact_flag
attributes.description: Boolean indicator of whether this deduction impacts trade promotion accruals. True indicates the deduction should be reconciled against accrued trade spend, false indicates it is outside accrual scope.

attributes.name: aging_days
attributes.description: Number of days the deduction has been open since the deduction date. Used for aging analysis and prioritization of dispute resolution activities.

attributes.name: amount
attributes.description: Total monetary amount deducted by the retailer from the invoice payment. Represents the initial claim amount before dispute resolution.

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date on which the deduction settlement was approved by the authorized approver. Represents the decision point in the workflow.

attributes.name: approved_amount
attributes.description: Monetary amount of the deduction that was approved and accepted by the manufacturer after dispute resolution. May be less than, equal to, or zero relative to the original deduction amount.

attributes.name: business_unit_code
attributes.description: Business unit or division code responsible for managing this deduction. Used for organizational reporting and accountability.

attributes.name: claim_reference_number
attributes.description: External reference number provided by the retailer for their deduction claim. Used for cross-referencing with retailer systems and documentation.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this deduction record was first created in the system. Represents the audit trail creation point.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the deduction amount. Indicates the currency in which the deduction was taken.

attributes.name: deduction_date
attributes.description: Date on which the retailer took the deduction from the invoice payment. Represents the business event timestamp for the deduction.

attributes.name: deduction_number
attributes.description: Business-facing unique deduction number assigned by the retailer or internal system. Used for external communication and dispute tracking.

attributes.name: deduction_type
attributes.description: Classification of the deduction based on the reason for the short-payment. Promotional deductions relate to trade promotions, pricing deductions to price discrepancies, shortage to quantity issues, compliance to retailer policy violations, quality to product defects, freight to shipping issues, and administrative to processing errors. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promotional|pricing|shortage|compliance|quality|freight|administrative — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: dispute_reason
attributes.description: Detailed textual explanation of why the manufacturer disputed all or part of the deduction. Provides context for dispute resolution and audit trail.

attributes.name: dispute_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the deduction in the dispute resolution workflow. Open indicates initial receipt, approved indicates full acceptance, disputed indicates challenge by manufacturer, partially_approved indicates partial acceptance, written_off indicates unrecoverable amount, and pending_review indicates under investigation.

attributes.name: disputed_amount
attributes.description: Monetary amount of the deduction that is currently under dispute or was rejected by the manufacturer. Represents the portion of the deduction not accepted.

attributes.name: gmroi_impact_percentage
attributes.business_glossary_term: Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI) Impact Percentage
attributes.description: Percentage impact of this deduction on the gross margin return on investment for the associated promotion. Used for profitability analysis and promotional effectiveness measurement.

tag_set.value: Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI) Impact Percentage

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this deduction record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

attributes.name: priority_level
attributes.description: Priority classification for dispute resolution based on deduction amount, retailer relationship, and aging. Critical indicates immediate attention required, high indicates urgent resolution needed, medium indicates standard processing, and low indicates routine handling.

attributes.name: resolution_notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes documenting the resolution process, communications with the retailer, and final settlement rationale. Provides audit trail and knowledge base for future similar deductions.

attributes.name: retailer_claim_date
attributes.description: Date on which the retailer filed the deduction claim in their system. May differ from the deduction date.

attributes.name: retailer_contact_email
attributes.description: Email address of the retailer contact person for this deduction. Used for electronic communication during dispute resolution.

attributes.name: retailer_contact_name
attributes.description: Name of the retailer contact person responsible for this deduction claim. Used for communication and relationship management during dispute resolution.

attributes.name: roi_impact_amount
attributes.description: Calculated impact of this deduction on the promotional return on investment. Represents the reduction in promotional effectiveness due to the deduction. Used for post-event analysis and promotional optimization.

attributes.name: settled_amount
attributes.description: Final monetary amount settled between the manufacturer and retailer after all dispute resolution activities. Represents the actual financial impact of the deduction.

attributes.name: settlement_date
attributes.description: Date on which the deduction was fully settled and closed. Represents the completion of the deduction lifecycle.

attributes.name: settlement_method
attributes.description: Method by which the deduction was settled. Credit memo indicates issuance of a credit note, check indicates physical or electronic check payment, offset_future_invoice indicates deduction from future invoices, write_off indicates unrecoverable amount expensed, and bank_transfer indicates direct electronic payment.

attributes.name: settlement_reason_code
attributes.description: Standardized code indicating the reason for the settlement decision. Used for categorization and analysis of settlement patterns.

attributes.name: sla_resolution_date
attributes.description: Target date by which the deduction should be resolved based on internal service level agreements or retailer contractual terms. Used for performance tracking and escalation management.

attributes.name: source
attributes.description: Source system or channel through which the deduction was received. Retailer portal indicates web-based submission, EDI 846 indicates electronic data interchange, manual entry indicates internal creation, bank reconciliation indicates discovery during payment matching, and automated matching indicates system-generated from remittance data.

attributes.name: supporting_document_url
attributes.description: URL or file path to supporting documentation for the deduction claim, such as proof of performance, promotional materials, or retailer correspondence. Used for audit and dispute resolution.

Enables closed-loop deduction management and accurate trade spend reconciliation.

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Deduction settlements impact brand and channel P&L and must be assigned to profit centers for management reporting. profit_center_code is a denormalized plain attribute. Replacing with a proper FK ena

attributes.name: trade_account_id
attributes.description: Reference to the retail customer account associated with this deduction settlement. Identifies the retailer or distributor party.

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: The date on which the settlement was formally approved by the authorized approver. Marks the transition from under review to approved status.

attributes.name: approved_amount
attributes.description: The amount approved by the manufacturer for settlement after review and validation. May differ from the claimed amount due to disputes or partial approvals.

attributes.name: business_unit_code

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this settlement record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for record creation.

attributes.name: payment_date

attributes.name: payment_reference_number
attributes.description: External reference number for the payment transaction, such as check number, wire transfer confirmation, or credit memo number. Used for reconciliation with financial systems.

attributes.name: settled_amount
