attributes.name: settlement_cycle_time_days
attributes.description: The number of days elapsed from deduction claim date to settlement completion. Used for process efficiency measurement and Service Level Agreement (SLA) tracking.

attributes.name: settlement_date
attributes.description: The date on which the deduction settlement was finalized and agreed upon by both parties. Represents the business event date for settlement completion.

attributes.name: settlement_method
attributes.description: The mechanism used to resolve and pay the deduction. Indicates how the settlement amount was transferred or credited to the retailer.

attributes.name: settlement_notes
attributes.description: Free-form text field for additional comments, negotiation details, or special circumstances related to the settlement. Provides context for future reference and audit.

attributes.name: settlement_number
attributes.description: Business-readable unique identifier for the settlement transaction. Used for external communication and audit trails. Format: STL- followed by 8-12 digits.

attributes.name: settlement_reason_code
attributes.description: Standardized code indicating the business reason or category for the settlement. Used for classification and root cause analysis of deductions.

attributes.name: settlement_reason_description
attributes.description: Detailed textual explanation of the reason for the settlement, including any specific circumstances, negotiations, or justifications documented during the settlement process.

attributes.name: settlement_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the deduction settlement. Tracks the settlement through approval, payment, and closure workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|approved|rejected|paid|cancelled|under_review|disputed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: sla_compliance_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this settlement was completed within the agreed Service Level Agreement (SLA) timeframe. Used for performance monitoring and retailer relationship management.

attributes.name: source_system_code
attributes.description: Identifier for the operational system from which this settlement record originated. Supports data lineage and multi-system integration scenarios.

attributes.name: source_system_record_code
attributes.description: The unique identifier of this settlement record in the source operational system. Enables traceability back to the system of record for reconciliation and troubleshooting.

products.name: promoted_price
products.description: Master entity capturing the promotional price points defined for SKUs within a promotion event or pricing program. Tracks promoted price type (Hi-Lo temporary price reduction, EDLP, multi-buy, BOGO, scan-back price), promoted price amount, regular shelf price (RSP/MSRP), price reduction depth (%), effective date range, retailer account, channel, and approval status. Distinct from standard list pricing — this entity owns the promotional price layer used during trade events.
products.reference: Promotional Price (SAP SD Condition Records, TPM)

attributes.name: promoted_price_id
attributes.description: Primary key for promoted_price

attributes.name: event_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent promotion event or campaign under which this promotional price is defined. Links to the broader trade promotion execution context.

attributes.name: price_list_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.price_list. Business justification: Promotional Price Calculation: promoted_price records a discount from a base price list. regular_price and regular_shelf_price are denormalized from price_list; linking to price_list enforces 3NF and

attributes.name: sku_id
attributes.description: Reference to the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for which this promotional price is defined. Identifies the specific product variant being promoted.

attributes.name: trade_account_id
attributes.description: Reference to the retailer or trade account for which this promotional price applies. Enables account-specific promotional pricing strategies.

attributes.name: trade_promotion_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to promotion.trade_promotion. Business justification: Promoted prices are defined within the context of a trade promotion program. While promoted_price already links to promotion_event (which links to trade_promotion), a direct FK to trade_promotion is n

attributes.name: accrual_method
attributes.description: The method used to accrue and settle trade promotion funds for this promotional price. Scan-back (per unit sold), bill-back (post-event invoice), off-invoice (upfront discount), lump-sum (fixed payment), or performance-based (tied to metrics).

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: The date when this promotional price was formally approved for execution. Part of the audit trail for trade promotion compliance.

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the promotional price record. Tracks the approval workflow from draft through execution to expiration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|rejected|active|expired|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: cost_of_goods_sold
attributes.description: The manufacturer's cost of goods sold per unit for the SKU at the time this promotional price was defined. Used for promotional ROI and GMROI analysis.

attributes.name: country_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where this promotional price is valid. Supports multi-country promotional planning and compliance.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this promotional pricing record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for data lineage and compliance.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the promotional price amount and regular shelf price (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: The date when this promotional price expires and is no longer valid. Nullable for open-ended or evergreen promotional pricing programs.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: The date when this promotional price becomes active and available for use in retail execution and point-of-sale systems.

attributes.name: estimated_volume_lift
attributes.description: The projected percentage increase in sales volume expected from this promotional price compared to baseline sales. Used for demand planning and promotional forecasting.

attributes.name: funding_source
attributes.description: Identifies the party responsible for funding this promotional price discount. Manufacturer-funded, retailer-funded, co-op (shared), vendor-funded, or third-party funded.

attributes.name: is_advertised
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this promotional price is featured in retailer advertising, circulars, or marketing campaigns. True if advertised, False if unadvertised in-store promotion.

attributes.name: is_stackable
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this promotional price can be combined (stacked) with other promotions, coupons, or discounts. True if stackable, False if exclusive.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this promotional pricing record was last updated or modified. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.

attributes.name: maximum_purchase_quantity
attributes.description: The maximum number of units eligible for this promotional price per transaction or per customer. Used to cap promotional exposure and manage trade spend. Nullable if no cap applies.

attributes.name: minimum_purchase_quantity
attributes.description: The minimum number of units a consumer or retailer must purchase to qualify for this promotional price. Used for multi-buy and volume-based promotions. Nullable if no minimum applies.

attributes.name: price_reduction_percentage
attributes.description: The percentage discount from the regular shelf price, calculated as ((regular_shelf_price - promotional_price_amount) / regular_shelf_price) * 100. Represents the depth of the promotional discount.

attributes.name: pricing_strategy_type
attributes.description: Classification of the promotional pricing strategy. Hi-Lo (High-Low temporary price reduction), EDLP (Everyday Low Price), multi-buy (e.g., 2 for $5), BOGO (Buy One Get One), scan-back (retailer scans at checkout for discount), temporary price reduction, rebate, or coupon-based pricing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hi_lo|edlp|multi_buy|bogo|scan_back|temporary_price_reduction|rebate|coupon — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: promotional_allowance_amount
attributes.description: The per-unit allowance or funding provided by the manufacturer to the retailer to support this promotional price. Used for trade spend accrual and settlement calculations.

attributes.name: promotional_price_amount
attributes.description: The promoted price point for the SKU during the promotional period. This is the actual selling price offered to consumers or retailers during the promotion.

attributes.name: promotional_price_code
attributes.description: Business identifier or code for this promotional price record. Used for external reference and integration with TPM systems.

attributes.name: promotional_price_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the promotional price offer, including terms, conditions, and any special instructions for retail execution or consumer communication.

attributes.name: promotional_price_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name or label for this promotional price configuration, used for identification and reporting purposes.

attributes.name: region_code
attributes.description: Geographic region code where this promotional price is applicable. Enables regional pricing strategies and localized trade promotions.

attributes.name: retailer_margin_percentage
attributes.description: The target or agreed-upon margin percentage for the retailer when selling at this promotional price. Used for trade negotiation and pricing strategy analysis.

attributes.name: scan_back_rate
attributes.description: The per-unit reimbursement rate paid to the retailer for each unit sold at the promotional price under a scan-back pricing model. Nullable if not a scan-back promotion.

attributes.name: tpm_system_code
attributes.description: External system identifier from the source Trade Promotion Management system (e.g., Salesforce TPM, SAP TPM). Used for data lineage and system integration.

products.name: funding_allocation
products.description: This association product represents the Contract between trade_promotion and funding_agreement. It captures the formal financial commitment that links a specific funding agreement to a specific trade promotion, recording how much of the agreement's budget is allocated to the promotion, at what percentage, and over what effective period. Each record links one trade_promotion to one funding_agreement with attributes (allocated_amount, allocation_percentage, funding_type, effective dates) that exist only in the context of this specific allocation relationship. Managed by brand managers and trade finance teams in TPM systems as a distinct operational entity.. Existence Justification: In Consumer Goods TPM, a trade promotion can draw funding from multiple funding agreements (e.g., an annual lump-sum RFA plus a scan-back agreement plus a co-op fund), and a single funding agreement (e.g., an annual JBP) can fund multiple distinct trade promotions across the year. Brand managers and trade finance teams actively manage these allocations — deciding how much of each agreement's budget is committed to each promotion — as a distinct operational process in systems like Salesforce TPM and SAP TPM. The relationship itself carries financial data (allocated_amount, allocation_percentage, funding_type) that belongs neither to the promotion nor the agreement alone, but to the specific funding commitment between them.

attributes.name: funding_allocation_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Promotion Funding Allocation - Promotion Funding Allocation Id
attributes.description: Primary key for the promotion_funding_allocation association

tag_set.value: Promotion Funding Allocation - Promotion Funding Allocation Id

attributes.name: funding_agreement_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Promotion Funding Allocation - Funding Agreement Id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to the funding agreement that is the source of funds for this allocation.

tag_set.value: Promotion Funding Allocation - Funding Agreement Id

attributes.name: trade_promotion_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Promotion Funding Allocation - Trade Promotion Id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to the trade promotion program that is receiving funding under this allocation.

tag_set.value: Promotion Funding Allocation - Trade Promotion Id

attributes.name: allocated_amount
attributes.description: The specific dollar amount of the funding agreement's budget committed to this trade promotion. This amount belongs to the allocation relationship, not to the promotion or agreement alone, as the same agreement may allocate different amounts to different promotions.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 currency; see currency_code on parent entities

attributes.name: allocation_percentage
attributes.description: The percentage of the funding agreement's total committed amount that is allocated to this specific trade promotion. Enables trade finance teams to track how agreement budgets are distributed across promotions.

attributes.name: allocation_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of this funding allocation: draft (being planned), approved (formally committed), active (funds being drawn), settled (fully reconciled), cancelled. Tracks the operational state of the commitment.

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: The date on which this funding allocation becomes operative and funds can begin to be drawn against the agreement for this promotion. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.

attributes.name: funding_period_end_date
attributes.description: The end date of the funding window for this specific promotion-agreement allocation. May be a subset of the parent agreement's overall funding period.

attributes.name: funding_period_start_date
attributes.description: The start date of the funding window for this specific promotion-agreement allocation. May be a subset of the parent agreement's overall funding period.

attributes.name: funding_type
attributes.description: The funding mechanism governing this specific allocation (e.g., off-invoice, scan-back, lump-sum, co-op). May differ from the parent agreement's overall funding_type when a multi-mechanism agreement funds a promotion through a specific channel.

domains.name: finance
domains.description: Owns financial planning, accounting, and reporting including general ledger, accounts payable/receivable, cost accounting, COGS allocation, EBITDA reporting, DSO/DIO tracking, budgeting, revenue recognition, and SOX-compliant financial controls and audit trails. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA FI/CO and Oracle Cloud Financials.

products.name: cost_center
products.description: Master record for organizational cost centers used in SAP S/4HANA CO module. Defines the financial accountability units within the CPG enterprise — brand units, manufacturing plants, regional sales offices, and shared service centers. Each cost center carries a hierarchy path, responsible manager, controlling area, profit center assignment, currency, and active period. Serves as the foundational dimension for all cost accounting, COGS allocation, and EBITDA reporting.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the cost center. Primary key for the cost center master record in SAP S/4HANA CO module.

attributes.name: activity_type_indicator
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this cost center performs activity-based costing. True if the center provides measurable activities (e.g., machine hours, labor hours) that are allocated to cost objects.

attributes.name: allocation_method
attributes.description: Method used for allocating costs from this cost center to other cost objects (products, profit centers). Critical for COGS calculation and overhead absorption.

attributes.name: budget_profile_code
attributes.description: Budget control profile defining budget planning rules, tolerance limits, and approval workflows for this cost center. Determines budget availability check behavior.

attributes.name: business_area_code
attributes.description: Business area classification for cross-company code reporting. Represents a distinct line of business or product category (e.g., Personal Care, Home Care, Beauty) for consolidated financial analysis.

attributes.name: category
attributes.description: Accounting classification indicating whether costs are direct (attributable to products), indirect (supporting operations), overhead (administrative), or capital (asset-related). Critical for COGS calculation and financial reporting.

attributes.name: code
attributes.description: Business identifier for the cost center. Alphanumeric code used across SAP S/4HANA FI/CO modules for financial accountability tracking. Typically follows organizational naming conventions (e.g., plant code + department code).

attributes.name: company_code
attributes.description: Legal entity company code in SAP S/4HANA FI module. Links cost center to the legal entity for statutory financial reporting and SOX compliance.

attributes.name: cost_center_group
attributes.description: Logical grouping of cost centers for consolidated reporting and analysis. Enables flexible aggregation beyond standard hierarchy (e.g., all brand marketing centers, all R&D centers).

attributes.name: cost_center_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the cost center. Active centers accept postings; inactive centers are closed; blocked centers are temporarily suspended; planned centers are approved but not yet operational.

attributes.name: cost_center_type
attributes.description: Classification of the cost center by functional area. Determines the nature of costs tracked and reporting hierarchy. Used for COGS allocation and EBITDA segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: production|sales|marketing|distribution|administration|research_development|quality_assurance|procurement|finance|shared_services — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: country_code
attributes.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the cost center operates. Supports multi-country financial consolidation and regulatory reporting.

attributes.name: created_by_user
attributes.description: User ID of the person who created the cost center master record. Audit trail for SOX compliance and data governance.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the cost center master record was created in the system. Audit trail for SOX compliance.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for cost center transactions. Defines the local currency for budget planning and actual cost recording.

attributes.name: department_code
attributes.description: Department or functional unit code within the organization. Provides granular classification for workforce planning and departmental cost analysis.

attributes.name: description
attributes.description: Extended description providing additional context about the cost center's purpose, scope, and responsibilities within the organization.

attributes.name: functional_area_code
attributes.description: Functional area classification for cost-of-sales accounting. Distinguishes between manufacturing, sales, marketing, R&D, and administrative functions for COGS and operating expense allocation.

attributes.name: hierarchy_area
attributes.description: Standard hierarchy assignment for organizational reporting. Defines the cost center's position in the corporate hierarchy tree for roll-up reporting and budget consolidation.

attributes.name: last_modified_by_user
attributes.description: User ID of the person who last modified the cost center master record. Audit trail for change tracking and SOX compliance.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the cost center master record was last modified. Audit trail for change tracking and SOX compliance.

attributes.name: location_code
attributes.description: Geographic location or site code for the cost center. Used for regional cost analysis and location-based reporting.

attributes.name: lock_indicator
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether the cost center is locked for actual postings. Used during period-end close and organizational changes to prevent unintended transactions.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Full descriptive name of the cost center. Human-readable label identifying the organizational unit (e.g., 'Brand Marketing - North America', 'Manufacturing Plant 01 - Production Line A').

attributes.name: parent_cost_center_code
attributes.description: Code of the parent cost center in the organizational hierarchy. Enables hierarchical roll-up of costs for management reporting and budget aggregation.

attributes.name: plant_code
attributes.description: Manufacturing plant or distribution center code associated with this cost center. Links production and logistics cost centers to physical facilities for COGS allocation.

attributes.name: record_version
attributes.description: Version number of the cost center master record. Incremented with each modification to support change history tracking and optimistic locking.

attributes.name: region_code
attributes.description: Geographic region classification (e.g., NOAM, EMEA, APAC, LATAM) for regional financial reporting and performance benchmarking.

attributes.name: source_system_code
attributes.description: Identifier of the source ERP system from which this cost center record originated. Supports multi-system landscapes and data lineage tracking.

attributes.name: statistical_indicator
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this is a statistical cost center used only for informational reporting without actual cost postings. Used for tracking non-financial metrics.

attributes.name: valid_from_date
attributes.description: Date from which the cost center is valid and can accept cost postings. Supports time-dependent organizational changes and fiscal year transitions.

attributes.name: valid_to_date
attributes.description: Date until which the cost center is valid. Nullable for open-ended cost centers. Used for organizational restructuring and cost center retirement.

products.name: profit_center
products.description: Master record for profit centers representing autonomous business segments within the consumer goods enterprise — product lines, brands, or geographic business units. Tracks revenue, cost, and profitability at a granular level below the legal entity. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA (Profit Center Accounting) and Oracle Cloud Financials for segment reporting and EBITDA decomposition.

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the profit center. Primary key for this entity representing an autonomous business segment within the consumer goods enterprise.

attributes.name: parent_profit_center_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent profit center in a hierarchical profit center structure. Enables roll-up reporting and EBITDA decomposition across organizational levels.

attributes.name: address_line_1
attributes.description: Primary street address line for the profit center's physical or administrative location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.

attributes.name: address_line_2
attributes.description: Secondary address line for suite, building, or department information. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.

attributes.name: brand_code
attributes.description: Brand identifier for brand-based profit centers. Enables brand-level P&L reporting and brand portfolio management.

attributes.name: business_area_code
attributes.description: Business area code representing a cross-company-code organizational unit for segment reporting. Enables consolidated financial statements by business segment.

attributes.name: channel_code
attributes.description: Sales channel classification for channel-based profit centers: retail, wholesale, e-commerce, direct-to-consumer (DTC), foodservice, or export.

attributes.name: city
attributes.description: City name for the profit center's location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.

attributes.name: code
attributes.description: Business identifier code for the profit center used in financial reporting and segment analysis. Externally-known unique code aligned with SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA master data.

attributes.name: company_code
attributes.description: Legal entity company code to which this profit center belongs. Used for statutory financial reporting and consolidation.

attributes.name: consolidation_unit_code
attributes.description: Code of the consolidation unit for group reporting. Links profit center to the financial consolidation hierarchy for IFRS/GAAP reporting.

attributes.name: cost_center_group_code
attributes.description: Code of the cost center group associated with this profit center. Links profit center accounting to cost center accounting for overhead allocation and COGS calculation.

attributes.name: country_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the profit center's location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this profit center record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the profit center's local reporting currency. Used for revenue and cost tracking before consolidation.

attributes.name: description
attributes.description: Detailed textual description of the profit center's business scope, responsibilities, and strategic objectives.

attributes.name: email_address
attributes.description: General email address for the profit center for business correspondence. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.

attributes.name: fax_number
attributes.description: Fax number for the profit center if applicable. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.

attributes.name: functional_area_code
attributes.description: Functional area code representing the business function (e.g., production, sales, administration) for cost-of-sales accounting and functional P&L reporting.

attributes.name: geographic_region_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country or region code representing the primary geographic location of the profit center for regional segment reporting.

attributes.name: hierarchy_level
attributes.description: Numeric level of this profit center within the organizational hierarchy. Level 1 represents top-level segments, with increasing numbers for deeper levels.

attributes.name: last_modified_by_user
attributes.description: Username or identifier of the user who last modified this profit center record. Audit trail for change accountability.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this profit center record was last updated. Audit trail for record modifications.

attributes.name: lock_indicator
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the profit center is locked for transaction postings. Used during period-end close and audit processes.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Full business name of the profit center representing the product line, brand, or geographic business unit.

attributes.name: phone_number
attributes.description: Primary contact phone number for the profit center. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.

attributes.name: planning_enabled_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this profit center participates in budgeting and financial planning processes.

attributes.name: postal_code
attributes.description: Postal or ZIP code for the profit center's location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.

attributes.name: product_category_code
attributes.description: Product category or product line code associated with this profit center. Used for product-based segment reporting and portfolio analysis.

attributes.name: profit_center_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the profit center indicating whether it is operational, temporarily suspended, or permanently closed.

attributes.name: profit_center_type
attributes.description: Classification of the profit center by organizational dimension: product line, brand, geographic region, sales channel, or business unit. Determines the segment reporting structure.

attributes.name: responsible_person_email
attributes.description: Email address of the profit center manager for financial reporting notifications and escalations.

attributes.name: responsible_person_name
attributes.description: Name of the business leader or manager accountable for the profit center's financial performance and operational results.

attributes.name: segment_code
attributes.description: Segment code for external segment reporting per IFRS 8 and US GAAP ASC 280. Represents reportable operating segments for investor disclosures.

attributes.name: short_name
attributes.description: Abbreviated name or acronym for the profit center used in reports and dashboards for space-constrained displays.

attributes.name: sox_control_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this profit center is subject to SOX financial controls and audit requirements for publicly traded companies.

attributes.name: state_province_code
attributes.description: State or province code for the profit center's location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.

attributes.name: tax_jurisdiction_code
attributes.description: Tax jurisdiction code for the profit center's location. Used for tax reporting and compliance with local tax authorities.

attributes.name: valid_from_date
attributes.description: Effective start date from which this profit center is active and available for transaction postings. Supports time-dependent organizational structures.

attributes.name: valid_to_date
attributes.description: Effective end date after which this profit center is no longer active. Null value indicates an open-ended validity period.

attributes.name: valuation_view
attributes.description: Valuation perspective for profit center accounting: legal (statutory), group (IFRS/GAAP consolidation), profit center (internal transfer pricing), or management (operational view).

products.name: gl_account
products.description: General ledger chart of accounts master for the consumer goods enterprise. Defines every account in the COA including P&L accounts (revenue, COGS, trade spend, marketing, SG&A), balance sheet accounts (inventory, AR, AP, fixed assets), and statistical accounts. Stores account type, financial statement category, tax category, reconciliation account flag, currency, and controlling relevance. Source of truth for all financial postings in SAP S/4HANA FI and Oracle Cloud Financials.

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the general ledger account. Primary key for the GL account master data.

attributes.name: chart_of_accounts_id
attributes.description: Reference to the chart of accounts framework to which this account belongs. Links to the chart_of_accounts master data.

attributes.name: company_code_id
attributes.description: Reference to the company code (legal entity) to which this GL account belongs. Links to the company_code master data.

attributes.name: account_group
attributes.description: The account group classification that controls field status, number ranges, and posting rules in the ERP system.

attributes.name: account_name
attributes.description: The full descriptive name of the GL account as it appears in financial reports and statements.

attributes.name: account_number
attributes.description: The externally-known unique account number in the chart of accounts. This is the business identifier used in financial postings and reporting.

attributes.name: account_short_name
attributes.description: Abbreviated name or label for the GL account used in condensed reports and system displays.

attributes.name: account_status
attributes.description: The current lifecycle status of the GL account: active (in use), inactive (not currently used but retained), pending approval (awaiting activation), or archived (historical only).

attributes.name: account_type
attributes.description: The fundamental accounting classification of the account: asset, liability, equity, revenue, expense, or statistical (non-monetary).

attributes.name: balance_sheet_classification
attributes.description: For balance sheet accounts, indicates whether the account is a current asset, non-current asset, current liability, non-current liability, or equity. Not applicable for P&L accounts.

attributes.name: blocked_for_posting_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the account is currently blocked for all postings. Used to prevent transactions to accounts that are under review, closed, or being restructured.

attributes.name: budget_control_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether budget availability checking is active for this account. When enabled, postings are validated against approved budgets.

attributes.name: cash_flow_category
attributes.description: For accounts that impact cash flow, indicates the cash flow statement category: operating activities, investing activities, or financing activities. Not applicable for non-cash accounts.

attributes.name: consolidation_account_number
attributes.description: The corresponding account number in the group consolidation chart of accounts. Used for mapping local accounts to group reporting structures.

attributes.name: cost_center_required_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether a cost center assignment is mandatory when posting to this account. Used for internal cost allocation and management reporting.

attributes.name: cost_element_category
attributes.description: For accounts that are cost elements, indicates whether it is a primary cost element (direct posting from FI) or secondary cost element (internal allocation only). Not applicable if not a cost element.

attributes.name: cost_element_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this GL account is also defined as a cost element in the Controlling (CO) module for cost center and internal order postings.

attributes.name: created_by_user
attributes.description: The user ID of the person who created this GL account record. Used for audit trail and accountability.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this GL account record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the account's local currency. For multi-currency accounts, this is the primary reporting currency.

attributes.name: debit_credit_indicator
attributes.description: Indicates whether the account normally carries a debit balance or credit balance in the general ledger.

attributes.name: field_status_group
attributes.description: The field status group that controls which fields are required, optional, or suppressed when posting to this account.

attributes.name: financial_statement_category
attributes.description: The primary financial statement where this account appears: balance sheet, income statement (P&L), cash flow statement, retained earnings, or statistical.

attributes.name: financial_statement_line_item
attributes.description: The specific line item or section within the financial statement where this account balance is reported (e.g., Current Assets, Cost of Goods Sold, Operating Expenses).

attributes.name: intercompany_clearing_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this account is used for intercompany transactions that require clearing and elimination in consolidated financial statements.

attributes.name: last_modified_by_user
attributes.description: The user ID of the person who last modified this GL account record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this GL account record was last modified.

attributes.name: line_item_display_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether individual line item details are stored and can be displayed for this account. Required for detailed transaction analysis and audit trails.

attributes.name: open_item_management_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this account uses open item management for tracking and clearing individual line items (e.g., AR, AP, bank clearing accounts).

attributes.name: pl_category
attributes.description: For income statement accounts, indicates the P&L category: gross revenue, net revenue, COGS, gross margin, operating expense (SG&A, marketing, trade spend), EBITDA, EBIT, or net income. Not applicable for balance sheet accounts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: gross_revenue|net_revenue|cogs|gross_margin|operating_expense|ebitda|ebit|net_income|not_applicable — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: planning_level
attributes.description: The organizational level at which budget planning and forecasting is performed for this account (e.g., company code, profit center, cost center).

attributes.name: posting_allowed_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether direct postings are allowed to this account. Some accounts (e.g., summary or control accounts) may only accept postings from automated processes.

attributes.name: profit_center_required_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether a profit center assignment is mandatory when posting to this account. Used for segment reporting and profitability analysis.

attributes.name: reconciliation_account_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this is a reconciliation account (subledger control account) that summarizes detailed transactions from subsidiary ledgers such as AR, AP, or inventory.

attributes.name: retained_earnings_account_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this is the retained earnings account where net income/loss is transferred during year-end closing.

attributes.name: sort_key
attributes.description: The default sort key used for organizing and displaying line items within this account (e.g., posting date, document number, amount).

attributes.name: sox_control_account_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this account is subject to SOX internal control requirements and enhanced audit procedures.

attributes.name: tax_category
attributes.description: The tax treatment category for this account (e.g., taxable revenue, non-taxable revenue, input tax, output tax, tax-exempt). Used for VAT, sales tax, and GST reporting.

attributes.name: tax_code_allowed_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether tax codes can be entered when posting to this account. Typically true for revenue and expense accounts, false for balance sheet accounts.

attributes.name: valid_from_date
attributes.description: The date from which this GL account is valid and available for posting. Used for time-dependent account structures.

attributes.name: valid_to_date
attributes.description: The date until which this GL account is valid. After this date, the account is no longer available for posting. Null indicates no end date.

products.name: ledger
products.description: Financial ledger master defining the accounting ledgers maintained in the enterprise — leading ledger (IFRS/GAAP), parallel ledgers for local GAAP, and extension ledgers for management reporting. Each ledger record captures ledger type, accounting principle, currency type, fiscal year variant, and posting period variant. Supports multi-GAAP reporting requirements common in global CPG companies operating across multiple regulatory jurisdictions.
products.reference: SAP FI Ledger / Oracle Cloud Ledger

attributes.name: ledger_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the financial ledger record. Primary key for the ledger entity.

attributes.name: chart_of_accounts_id
attributes.description: FK to finance.chart_of_accounts

attributes.name: company_code_id
attributes.description: FK to finance.company_code

attributes.name: accounting_principle
attributes.description: The accounting standard or principle applied in this ledger, such as International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS), United States Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (US GAAP), local country-specific GAAP, tax accounting rules, or management accounting conventions.

attributes.name: audit_trail_enabled
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether comprehensive audit trail logging is enabled for all postings and changes to this ledger, supporting regulatory compliance and forensic analysis.

attributes.name: base_currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the base currency in which this ledger maintains balances (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY).

attributes.name: code
attributes.description: Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the ledger within the enterprise. Used as the business identifier for the ledger in SAP S/4HANA FI and Oracle Cloud Financials.

attributes.name: consolidation_ledger_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this ledger is used for group consolidation purposes, aggregating financial data from multiple legal entities for consolidated financial statements.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp recording when this ledger record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for ledger lifecycle management.

attributes.name: currency_type
attributes.description: Defines the currency perspective maintained in this ledger. Local currency represents the operating entity's functional currency, group currency is the consolidated reporting currency, hard currency is a stable reference currency (e.g., USD, EUR), and index currency supports inflation-adjusted reporting.

attributes.name: description
attributes.description: Detailed textual description of the ledger's purpose, scope, and usage within the enterprise financial reporting architecture. Provides context for finance users and auditors.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: The date on which this ledger ceases to be active and no longer accepts financial postings. Null for ledgers with indefinite validity.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: The date from which this ledger becomes active and begins accepting financial postings. Supports temporal validity for ledger lifecycle management.

attributes.name: fiscal_year_variant
attributes.description: Code identifying the fiscal year calendar structure used by this ledger, defining the number of posting periods, special periods, and year-end closing rules. Common in SAP S/4HANA FI to support diverse fiscal calendars across global entities.

attributes.name: is_leading_ledger
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this is the leading ledger (primary book of record) for the company code. Only one ledger per company code can be designated as the leading ledger.

attributes.name: last_modified_by
attributes.description: User identifier or name of the person who last modified this ledger record. Supports change tracking and audit trail for ledger configuration.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp recording when this ledger record was last modified. Enables change tracking and supports audit trail requirements for financial system configuration.

attributes.name: ledger_status
attributes.description: Current operational status of the ledger. Active ledgers accept postings, inactive ledgers are configured but not yet in use, closed ledgers are archived and no longer accept postings, and suspended ledgers are temporarily disabled.

attributes.name: ledger_type
attributes.description: Classification of the ledger indicating its role in the financial reporting architecture. Leading ledger is the primary book of record, parallel ledgers support alternative accounting principles (e.g., local GAAP), and extension ledgers provide management reporting views.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Full descriptive name of the ledger for human-readable identification and reporting purposes.

attributes.name: posting_period_variant
attributes.description: Code defining the posting period structure for this ledger, including the number of regular periods (typically 12 monthly periods) and special periods for year-end adjustments and closing entries.

attributes.name: retention_period_years
attributes.description: Number of years that financial data in this ledger must be retained to comply with statutory, tax, and regulatory record-keeping requirements. Common retention periods range from 7 to 10 years for financial records.

attributes.name: sox_compliant_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this ledger maintains controls and audit trails compliant with Sarbanes-Oxley Act Section 404 requirements for internal controls over financial reporting.

attributes.name: supports_cost_of_sales_accounting
attributes.business_glossary_term: Supports Cost of Sales (COGS) Accounting Flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this ledger supports cost of sales (COGS) accounting method, which matches costs directly to revenue in the period of sale, as opposed to period-based cost accounting.

tag_set.value: Supports Cost of Sales (COGS) Accounting Flag

attributes.name: supports_profit_center_accounting
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this ledger maintains profit center accounting data for internal management reporting and profitability analysis by organizational unit.

attributes.name: supports_segment_reporting
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this ledger supports segment reporting as required by IFRS 8 Operating Segments. Segment reporting enables financial analysis by business unit, product line, or geographic region.

attributes.name: valuation_view
attributes.description: Defines the valuation perspective applied in this ledger. Legal valuation follows statutory requirements, group valuation applies consolidated group policies, profit center valuation supports internal management reporting, and cost center valuation tracks cost allocation.

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: User identifier or name of the person who created this ledger record in the system. Supports audit trail and accountability for ledger configuration changes.

products.name: journal_entry
products.description: Core general ledger journal entry header capturing every financial posting in the consumer goods enterprise. Records document type (vendor invoice, customer payment, goods issue, payroll posting, accrual, reversal), posting date, fiscal period, company code, ledger, currency, exchange rate, reference document, and SOX-compliant posting user and approval chain. The authoritative transactional record for all financial activity sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI and Oracle Cloud Financials.

attributes.name: journal_entry_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the journal entry record. Primary key for the journal entry header.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the cost center to which costs or revenues from this journal entry are allocated. Used for management accounting and EBITDA reporting.

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Journal entries reference GL accounts by code; adding gl_account_id FK normalizes the relationship and removes the redundant code column.

attributes.name: ledger_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Journal entries belong to a specific ledger; adding ledger_id FK links each entry to its ledger without removing existing ledger_group (kept for reporting).

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the profit center for segment reporting and profitability analysis. Used for EBITDA calculation by business unit.

attributes.name: approval_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the journal entry was approved. Part of the SOX-compliant audit trail. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

attributes.name: baseline_payment_date
attributes.description: The baseline date used for calculating payment terms and due dates for vendor invoices and customer receivables. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.

attributes.name: batch_input_session
attributes.description: Identifier of the batch input session if this journal entry was created through automated batch processing. Used for tracking mass data uploads and interface postings.

attributes.name: business_area_code
attributes.description: Code representing the business area or division for cross-company code reporting. Used for consolidated financial statements.

attributes.name: clearing_date
attributes.description: The date on which this open item was cleared. Used for DSO and DIO calculation. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.

attributes.name: clearing_document_number
attributes.description: Document number of the clearing document if this open item has been cleared (paid or offset). Used for accounts payable and receivable reconciliation.

attributes.name: company_code
attributes.description: Four-character alphanumeric code identifying the legal entity or business unit within the enterprise. Represents the smallest organizational unit for which a complete self-contained set of accounts can be drawn up for purposes of external reporting.

attributes.name: controlling_area
attributes.description: Organizational unit in management accounting that represents a closed system for cost accounting. Used for internal management reporting.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this journal entry record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the document currency in which the journal entry amounts are denominated. Examples: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY.

attributes.name: document_date
attributes.description: The date printed on the source document (invoice, receipt, payment voucher). May differ from posting date. Used for document control and audit trail. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.

attributes.name: document_number
attributes.description: Unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to the accounting document within the company code and fiscal year. This is the externally visible business key for the journal entry.

attributes.name: document_type
attributes.description: Classification code indicating the nature and source of the journal entry. Examples: SA (General Ledger Document), AB (Accounting Document), KR (Vendor Invoice), DR (Customer Invoice), DZ (Customer Payment), KA (Vendor Payment), KG (Vendor Credit Memo), KN (Customer Credit Memo), KZ (Payment Request), RE (Invoice Receipt), RV (Invoice Reversal), WA (Goods Issue), WE (Goods Receipt), ZP (Payment Posting). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SA|AB|KR|DR|DZ|KA|KG|KN|KZ|RE|RV|WA|WE|ZP — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: entry_date
attributes.description: The date on which the journal entry was created or entered into the system. Used for audit trail and process monitoring. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.

attributes.name: exchange_rate
attributes.description: The exchange rate used to convert document currency amounts to local currency. Expressed as local currency per one unit of document currency. Null if document currency equals local currency.

attributes.name: exchange_rate_type
attributes.description: Code indicating the type of exchange rate used for currency translation. Examples: M (Average Rate), B (Bank Buying Rate), G (Bank Selling Rate), P (Posting Date Rate).

attributes.name: fiscal_period
attributes.description: Numeric period within the fiscal year (typically 1-12 for monthly periods, or 1-13 for year-end adjustments). Determines the reporting period for financial statements.

attributes.name: fiscal_year
attributes.description: Four-digit year representing the fiscal year to which this journal entry is assigned. Used for period-based financial reporting and year-end closing.

attributes.name: functional_area_code
attributes.description: Code classifying the journal entry by functional area (e.g., Production, Sales, Administration, R&D). Used for cost of goods sold (COGS) allocation and functional expense reporting.

attributes.name: header_text
attributes.description: Free-form text field for additional information or explanation about the journal entry. Used for documentation and audit purposes.

attributes.name: intercompany_indicator
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry involves intercompany transactions between different company codes within the enterprise. True if intercompany, False otherwise.

attributes.name: ledger_group
attributes.description: Two-character code identifying the ledger or accounting principle under which this entry is recorded. Examples: 0L (Leading Ledger - IFRS), 2L (Local GAAP), 3L (Management Reporting). Supports parallel accounting for multiple reporting standards.

attributes.name: local_currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the company code local currency. All amounts are translated to this currency for statutory reporting.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this journal entry record was last modified. Part of the audit trail for tracking changes. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

attributes.name: payment_terms_code
attributes.description: Code representing the payment terms applicable to this document (e.g., Net 30, 2/10 Net 30). Used for cash flow forecasting and DSO tracking.

attributes.name: posting_date
attributes.description: The date on which the journal entry is posted to the general ledger. This is the effective date for financial reporting and determines the fiscal period assignment. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.

attributes.name: posting_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the journal entry. Values: draft (entry created but not posted), posted (entry successfully posted to GL), parked (entry saved for later posting), cancelled (entry voided before posting), reversed (entry reversed by a subsequent document).

attributes.name: reference_document_number
attributes.description: External reference number from the source document (invoice number, purchase order number, payment reference). Used for cross-referencing and reconciliation.

attributes.name: reversal_indicator
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry is a reversal of a previous entry. True if this is a reversal document, False otherwise.

attributes.name: reversal_reason_code
attributes.description: Code indicating the reason for reversing the original document. Examples: 01 (Reversal in Current Period), 02 (Reversal in Next Period), 03 (Correction), 04 (Accrual Reversal), 05 (Error Correction), 06 (Period-End Adjustment). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 01|02|03|04|05|06|07|08|09 — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: reversed_document_number
attributes.description: Document number of the original journal entry that is being reversed by this entry. Populated only when reversal_indicator is True.

attributes.name: source_system_code
attributes.description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this journal entry originated. Examples: SAP_FI (SAP S/4HANA Financial Accounting), SAP_CO (SAP Controlling), ORACLE_GL (Oracle Cloud General Ledger), ORACLE_AP (Oracle Accounts Payable), ORACLE_AR (Oracle Accounts Receivable), SFDC (Salesforce), WMS (Warehouse Management System), MES (Manufacturing Execution System). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SAP_FI|SAP_CO|ORACLE_GL|ORACLE_AP|ORACLE_AR|SFDC|WMS|MES — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: sox_control_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry is subject to SOX internal control testing and audit procedures. True if SOX-controlled, False otherwise.

attributes.name: tax_reporting_date
attributes.description: The date relevant for tax reporting purposes. May differ from posting date based on tax jurisdiction rules. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.

attributes.name: trading_partner_company_code
attributes.description: Company code of the intercompany trading partner if this is an intercompany transaction. Used for consolidation and elimination entries. Null if not an intercompany transaction.

attributes.name: transaction_code
attributes.description: SAP transaction code or Oracle program identifier used to create this journal entry. Examples: FB01 (Post Document), FB50 (G/L Account Posting), MIRO (Enter Invoice), F-28 (Post Incoming Payments). Used for audit trail and process analysis.

products.name: journal_entry_line
products.description: Individual line items of a general ledger journal entry. Each line captures the GL account, cost center, profit center, WBS element, business area, debit/credit indicator, amount in transaction currency, amount in company code currency, amount in group currency, tax code, and assignment field. Enables granular cost allocation, COGS decomposition, and audit trail tracing at the line level as required by SOX compliance.
products.reference: SAP FI Journal Entry Line Item (BSEG)

attributes.name: journal_entry_line_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the journal entry line item. Primary key for granular cost allocation, COGS decomposition, and SOX-compliant audit trail tracing at the line level.

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Journal entry lines store GL account code; FK to gl_account enables proper account lookup and eliminates duplicate code.

attributes.name: journal_cost_object_cost_center_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Journal Cost Object Cost Center Id

tag_set.value: Journal Cost Object Cost Center Id

attributes.name: journal_entry_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent journal entry header document. Links this line to the overall journal entry batch and posting context.

attributes.name: production_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: Journal entry lines for WIP, production order settlement, and variance postings reference production orders as cost objects. internal_order_number is a denormalized text field; proper FK enables CO-PA

attributes.name: amount_company_code_currency
attributes.description: Monetary amount converted to the local currency of the company code. Used for statutory reporting and local GAAP compliance.

attributes.name: amount_group_currency
attributes.description: Monetary amount converted to the group reporting currency. Enables consolidated financial reporting across all subsidiaries.

attributes.name: amount_transaction_currency
attributes.description: Monetary amount of this line item in the original transaction currency. Preserves the original posting amount before currency conversion.

attributes.name: assignment_field
attributes.description: Free-text assignment field for additional reference information such as invoice number, purchase order, or payment reference. Enhances traceability.

attributes.name: baseline_payment_date
attributes.description: Baseline date for payment terms calculation. Used to compute due dates and cash discount periods.

attributes.name: business_area_code
attributes.description: Business area classification for cross-company code segment reporting. Enables consolidated financial statements by business segment.

attributes.name: clearing_date
attributes.description: Date on which this open item was cleared or settled. Null if the item remains open.

attributes.name: clearing_document_number
attributes.description: Document number of the clearing entry that settled this open item. Null if the item remains open.

attributes.name: company_code
attributes.description: Legal entity identifier representing the company or subsidiary for which this line is posted. Enables consolidated and statutory reporting.

attributes.name: company_code_currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the company code local currency. Typically the statutory reporting currency of the legal entity.

attributes.name: customer_account_number
attributes.description: Customer account number for revenue and receivables postings. Links financial transactions to customer master data.

attributes.name: debit_credit_indicator
attributes.description: Indicates whether this line is a debit (D) or credit (C) posting. Fundamental to double-entry bookkeeping and account balance calculation.

attributes.name: due_date
attributes.description: Date by which payment is due for payables or expected for receivables. Critical for DSO and cash flow management.

attributes.name: exchange_rate
attributes.description: Exchange rate applied to convert transaction currency to company code or group currency. Critical for currency translation audit trail.

attributes.name: functional_area_code
attributes.description: Functional area classification (e.g., production, sales, administration, R&D). Supports cost-of-sales accounting and functional expense reporting.

attributes.name: group_currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the group reporting currency. Typically USD or EUR for multinational consumer goods companies.

attributes.name: line_item_number
attributes.description: Sequential line number within the journal entry header. Determines the ordering and position of this line in the document.

attributes.name: material_number
attributes.description: Material or SKU associated with this line item for inventory and COGS postings. Links financial postings to product master data.

attributes.name: payment_terms_code
attributes.description: Payment terms applicable to this line item. Determines due date calculation and cash discount eligibility.

attributes.name: plant_code
attributes.description: Manufacturing plant or distribution center associated with this line item. Supports plant-level cost accounting and inventory management.

attributes.name: posting_key
attributes.description: Two-digit posting key controlling account type, debit/credit indicator, and field status. Fundamental to SAP FI posting logic.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: Quantity of material or units associated with this line item. Enables unit cost calculation and inventory valuation.

attributes.name: reference_document_number
attributes.description: External or internal document number referenced by this line (e.g., invoice, purchase order, contract). Supports document linkage and reconciliation.

attributes.name: reversal_indicator
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this line item is a reversal of a previous posting. True if this is a reversal entry.

attributes.name: reversed_document_number
attributes.description: Document number of the original entry that this line reverses. Null if this is not a reversal.

attributes.name: special_gl_indicator
attributes.description: Special GL indicator for down payments, bills of exchange, guarantees, or other special transactions. Enables special ledger processing.

attributes.name: storage_location_code
attributes.description: Storage location within the plant for inventory-related postings. Enables warehouse-level inventory tracking and valuation.

attributes.name: tax_amount
attributes.description: Tax amount calculated for this line item in transaction currency. Supports VAT, sales tax, and other indirect tax reporting.

attributes.name: tax_code
attributes.description: Tax code determining the tax treatment of this line item. Drives automatic tax calculation and tax reporting compliance.

attributes.name: text_description
attributes.description: Descriptive text explaining the nature or purpose of this journal entry line. Provides human-readable context for audit and review.

attributes.name: trading_partner_code
attributes.description: Identifier of the intercompany trading partner for intercompany transactions. Enables intercompany reconciliation and elimination entries.

attributes.name: transaction_currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the transaction amount. Identifies the currency in which the original transaction was denominated.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the quantity field (e.g., EA, KG, LT). Standardizes quantity representation across transactions.

attributes.name: value_date
attributes.description: Value date for cash management and bank reconciliation. Represents the effective date for interest calculation and cash position.

attributes.name: vendor_account_number
attributes.description: Vendor account number for procurement and payables postings. Links financial transactions to vendor master data.

attributes.name: wbs_element_code
attributes.description: WBS element for project-related postings. Links costs and revenues to specific projects, phases, or deliverables in project accounting.

products.name: ar_invoice
products.description: Accounts receivable master record representing the full trade receivable lifecycle for consumer goods sales to retailers, distributors, and wholesalers. Owns invoice creation, payment terms, cash receipt application, partial payments, deductions/short-payments, clearing, and write-offs. Captures invoice number, billing date, due date, payment terms, gross amount, trade discounts, net amount, tax, currency, payment method, payment date, amount received, clearing reference, DSO aging bucket, dunning level, and deduction reason codes. The single source of truth for all receivable balances, cash application, deduction management, DSO tracking, cash flow forecasting, and revenue recognition under ASC 606/IFRS 15. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA SD billing and FI-AR.

attributes.name: ar_invoice_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the accounts receivable invoice record. Primary key for the AR invoice entity.

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: AR invoices reference GL accounts by code; adding FK normalizes accounting data.

attributes.name: sales_invoice_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.invoice. Business justification: AR invoice reconciliation process matches each AR invoice to the originating sales invoice for cash application.

attributes.name: trade_account_id
attributes.description: Reference to the trade account (customer) to whom this invoice was issued. Links to the customer master record.

attributes.name: amount_received
attributes.description: Total amount received from the customer against this invoice. May be less than total amount due to partial payments or deductions. Null for unpaid invoices.

attributes.name: billing_date
attributes.description: Date when the invoice was created and issued to the customer. Principal business event timestamp for the invoice lifecycle.

attributes.name: billing_document_type
attributes.description: SAP billing document type code indicating the nature of the invoice (e.g., F2=standard invoice, G2=credit memo, L2=debit memo). Determines accounting treatment and revenue recognition.

attributes.name: clearing_date
attributes.description: Date when the invoice was cleared in the financial system, either through full payment or write-off. Null for open invoices.

attributes.name: clearing_reference
attributes.description: Reference number assigned when the invoice is cleared in the financial system. Links to the clearing document for audit trail. Null for open invoices.

attributes.name: company_code
attributes.description: SAP company code representing the legal entity that issued the invoice. Required for multi-entity financial consolidation and statutory reporting.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: System timestamp when the invoice record was first created in the AR system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the invoice is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Critical for multi-currency reporting and foreign exchange management.

attributes.name: deduction_amount
attributes.description: Total amount of deductions taken by the customer against this invoice. Includes short-payments, chargebacks, and unauthorized deductions requiring resolution.

attributes.name: deduction_reason_code
attributes.description: Code indicating the reason for customer deductions (e.g., pricing dispute, damaged goods, short shipment, promotional allowance). Used for deduction root cause analysis.

attributes.name: dispute_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the invoice is currently under dispute by the customer. True if disputed, false otherwise. Disputed invoices are excluded from standard collection processes.

attributes.name: dispute_reason
attributes.description: Free-text description of the reason the customer is disputing the invoice. Used for dispute resolution and root cause analysis.

attributes.name: distribution_channel
attributes.description: Channel through which the goods were sold (e.g., retail, wholesale, DTC, e-commerce). Used for channel profitability analysis.

attributes.name: dso_aging_bucket
attributes.description: Aging category based on number of days the invoice has been outstanding past the due date. Used for DSO reporting, collections prioritization, and bad debt provisioning.

attributes.name: due_date
attributes.description: Date by which payment is expected from the customer based on agreed payment terms. Used for Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) calculation and dunning.

attributes.name: dunning_level
attributes.description: Current dunning level indicating the escalation stage of collection efforts (0=no dunning, 1=first reminder, 2=second reminder, etc.). Higher levels indicate more aggressive collection actions.

attributes.name: fiscal_period
attributes.description: Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year when the invoice was posted. Used for monthly financial reporting and trend analysis.

attributes.name: fiscal_year
attributes.description: Fiscal year in which the invoice was posted. Used for year-over-year financial analysis and annual reporting.

attributes.name: gl_posting_date
attributes.description: Date when the invoice was posted to the general ledger. Used for financial period assignment and month-end close processes.

attributes.name: gross_amount
attributes.description: Total invoice amount before any trade discounts, deductions, or adjustments. Represents the full billed value of goods or services.

attributes.name: invoice_number
attributes.description: Externally-known unique invoice number assigned by the billing system. Used for customer communication and payment reference.

attributes.name: invoice_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the invoice in the accounts receivable process. Tracks progression from open through payment to clearing or write-off. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|partially_paid|paid|cleared|written_off|disputed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: last_dunning_date
attributes.description: Date when the most recent dunning notice was sent to the customer. Used to track collection activity frequency and determine next dunning action.

attributes.name: modified_by_user
attributes.description: User ID of the person who last modified the invoice record. Required for SOX compliance and segregation of duties controls.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: System timestamp when the invoice record was last modified. Used for change tracking and SOX compliance audit trails.

attributes.name: net_amount
attributes.description: Invoice amount after trade discounts but before tax. This is the base amount subject to taxation and the primary value for revenue recognition.

attributes.name: outstanding_balance
attributes.description: Remaining unpaid balance on the invoice. Calculated as total amount minus amount received. Zero when invoice is fully paid or cleared.

attributes.name: payment_date
attributes.description: Date when payment was received from the customer. Null for unpaid invoices. Used for DSO calculation and cash flow analysis.

attributes.name: payment_method
attributes.description: Method by which the customer is expected to remit payment. Used for cash application automation and payment reconciliation.

attributes.name: payment_terms_code
attributes.description: Code representing the payment terms agreed with the customer (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30). Determines due date calculation and early payment discount eligibility.

attributes.name: revenue_recognition_date
attributes.description: Date when revenue from this invoice is recognized in the financial statements under ASC 606/IFRS 15. May differ from billing date based on performance obligation fulfillment.

attributes.name: sales_order_number
attributes.description: Reference to the originating sales order that generated this invoice. Links invoice to order fulfillment and enables order-to-cash traceability.

attributes.name: sales_organization
attributes.description: SAP sales organization responsible for the sale. Used for sales performance reporting and commission calculation.

attributes.name: shipment_number
attributes.description: Reference to the shipment or delivery document associated with this invoice. Links billing to physical goods movement for OTIF tracking.

attributes.name: tax_amount
attributes.description: Total tax amount charged on the invoice, including sales tax, VAT, GST, or other applicable indirect taxes based on jurisdiction.

attributes.name: total_amount
attributes.description: Final invoice amount including all discounts and taxes. This is the total amount due from the customer and the target for cash application.

attributes.name: trade_discount_amount
attributes.description: Total trade discounts applied to the invoice, including volume discounts, promotional allowances, and negotiated rebates. Reduces gross amount to net amount.

attributes.name: write_off_amount
attributes.description: Amount written off as uncollectible bad debt. Null for invoices not written off. Impacts bad debt expense and allowance for doubtful accounts.

attributes.name: write_off_date
attributes.description: Date when the invoice or portion thereof was written off as uncollectible. Null for invoices not written off.

attributes.name: write_off_reason_code
attributes.description: Code indicating the reason for write-off (e.g., customer bankruptcy, uncollectible, small balance write-off). Used for bad debt analysis and credit policy refinement.

products.name: ar_payment
products.description: Accounts receivable payment record capturing cash receipts from trade customers against outstanding AR invoices. Records payment date, payment method (ACH, wire, check, EDI 820), amount received, currency, exchange rate, bank account, clearing document reference, partial payment flag, and deduction/short-payment details. Supports cash application, deduction management, and DSO calculation. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI-AR and Oracle Cloud Receivables.

attributes.name: ar_payment_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the accounts receivable payment record. Primary key.

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: AR payments store GL account code; FK to gl_account consolidates GL reference.

attributes.name: invoice_id

attributes.name: created_timestamp

attributes.name: discount_amount
attributes.description: Cash discount or early payment discount amount taken by customer. Typically based on payment terms (e.g., 2/10 net 30).

attributes.name: due_date

attributes.name: edi_transaction_set_reference

attributes.name: exchange_rate

attributes.name: fiscal_year

attributes.name: local_currency_amount
attributes.description: Payment amount converted to company local currency using the exchange rate. Used for consolidated financial reporting.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the payment record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

attributes.name: overpayment_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether payment amount exceeds the invoice balance. True if customer paid more than owed.

attributes.name: partial_payment_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this is a partial payment against the invoice. True if payment amount is less than invoice balance.

attributes.name: payment_amount
attributes.description: Total amount received in the payment transaction, in the payment currency. Gross payment before any deductions or adjustments.

attributes.name: payment_channel

attributes.name: payment_currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the payment amount. Identifies the currency in which payment was received.

attributes.name: payment_date
attributes.description: Date when the payment was received or posted. Used for Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) calculation and cash flow analysis.

attributes.name: payment_document_number

attributes.name: payment_processor_code

attributes.name: value_date

products.name: ap_invoice
products.description: Accounts payable master record representing the full trade payable lifecycle for raw materials, packaging, contract manufacturing, and indirect goods/services. Owns invoice receipt, three-way match verification, payment scheduling, payment execution, early payment discount capture, and clearing. Captures vendor invoice number, invoice date, posting date, due date, gross amount, tax, net amount, payment terms, currency, match status, payment method, payment date, payment amount, payment run ID, bank account reference, early discount taken, clearing document, and payment block details. The single source of truth for all payable balances, payment execution, DIO tracking, working capital optimization, and supplier payment management. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA MM-IV, FI-AP, and Oracle Cloud Payables.
